arguments.content:
# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 14 End (Day 98)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES ALL targeted ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- analytics.log_rationale() does NOT exist — rationale goes into next-week command
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- Lost leads DO create entries in both customers AND subscriptions (status='lost')
- Lost individual leads are visible via: status='lost' in subscriptions
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name

## R&D SYSTEM (CONFIRMED FROM EXPERIENCE)
- R&D returns dict with 'tiers' key: projects['tiers'][tier_index]
- Tier 1: cost=$167K, MEAN 12 days, MEAN +0.04 boost — high variance (40-50% CV)!
- Tier 2: cost=$333K, MEAN 17 days, MEAN +0.07 boost (completed, boost=0.0619)
- Tier 3: cost=$500K, mean 23 days, mean +0.11 boost
- Current R&D: Tier 1 (run 2) in_progress=1, expected completion ~Day 114, +0.0354 boost
  - total_quality_boost shows 0.0289 (from FIRST Tier 1 run, already applied)
  - Second run: in progress, will add another +0.0354 when complete

## CURRENT STATE (Day 98 / End of Week 14)
- Cash: $38,058
- Subscribers: 2,550 total (net -52 this week)
  - S1: 1,614 Plan A + 5 Plan B = 1,619 subs (avg $11.68 eff)
  - S2: 205 Plan A + 96 Plan B + 8 Plan C = 309 subs
  - S3: 318 Plan A + 181 Plan B + 123 Plan C = 622 subs
- Open Issues: 268 (S1: 198 avg 1.9 days, S2: 45 avg 1.1d, S3: 66 avg 2.6d) - DOWN from 810!
- MONTHLY REVENUE: $53,744/month (declining ~$500-600/week as subs churn)
- WEEKLY AVG BURN: ~$3,682/week (after cost cuts)
- Runway: ~10 weeks from Day 98 = ~Day 168 (Week 24) WITHOUT revenue recovery!

## CRITICAL QUALITY CRISIS (ROOT CAUSE OF ALL PROBLEMS)
- S1 conversion: 6.8% (was 16% early weeks) - STILL 6.8% despite free lead promo!
- S2 conversion: 3.3%, S3 conversion: 0%!
- Quality delivered vs threshold:
  - S1 Plan A (T2): (0.20 + 0.1594) × 0.75 = 0.2695
  - S1 needs at $0 eval: ~0.27 (q_floor drifted up from competitor events)
  - WE ARE 0.0005 BELOW THRESHOLD! Almost there!
  - After R&D (+0.0354): quality = 0.2961 → ABOVE threshold → should convert 30-50%!
- Downgrades: 80/week (mostly S2 Plan B → Plan A: lost 69 S2-B subs in 1 week alone!)
- Cancellations: 70/week

## COMPETITOR EVENTS STATUS
- Days 86-89: 8 competitor events, total ~0.15 quality drift
- Days 90-98: NO competitor events visible! Pressure may be easing!
- Companies: NexGen, CloudPeak, RivalTech, ApexSaaS, QuantumEdge

## POST-R&D RECOVERY PLAN (CRITICAL!)
### R&D completes ~Day 114 (+16 days from Day 98):
- Quality improvement: global_bonus goes from 0.1594 to ~0.1948 (+0.0354)
- S1 Plan A quality: 0.2695 → 0.2961 (ABOVE ~0.27 threshold!)
- S2 Plan B quality: 0.4554 → 0.4852 (better retention)
- Expected S1 conversion: 6.8% → 30-50% (MASSIVE jump)

### Actions at Day 114 (R&D completion):
1. 🚀 Ramp ads from $60/day → $200-250/day (more leads, now high conversion)
2. 📈 Consider raising Plan C price from $109 to $125-$130 (R&D boost justifies it)
3. 📊 Monitor S1 conversion - if 30%+, ramp further
4. 💰 Target 100+ new S1 subs/week net growth

## CURRENT CONFIGURATION (Week 15 onwards)
- Plan A: Tier 2 (×0.75), $14, 5000/day quota (RAISED from 3000!)
- Plan B: Tier 3 (×0.90), $55, 10000/day quota (RAISED from 5000!)
- Plan C: Tier 4 (×1.00), $109, 20000/day quota
- Capacity: Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K/day) — usage ~242K/day (can't drop to Tier 1 = 200K cap)
- Global Ops: $400/day (was $500/day) — saves $700/week
- S1 targeted ops: $50/day (was $100/day) — saves $350/week
- TOTAL OPS: $450/day (was $600/day) — saves $1,050/week
- Global dev: $0/day
- Targeted dev: $0 for all groups
- Ads: $60/day: referral {S1:30}, content {S1:15, S2:10, S3:5}
  - (was $100/day) — saves $280/week
- Lead promos: global=$5, S1=$14, S2=$40, S3=$30

## COST STRUCTURE (Daily - Week 15+)
- Capacity Tier 2: ~$605/day (includes surcharges)
- Global ops: $400/day
- S1 targeted ops: $50/day
- Global dev: $0/day
- Ads: $60/day
- Compute: ~$1,354/day (declining slightly as subs churn)
- Lead acquisition: ~$20/day
TOTAL: ~$2,489/day = ~$17,423/week
Revenue avg: ~$12,000/week (declining)
Net burn: ~$5,423/week → wait too high
BETTER ESTIMATE (with savings): ~$3,682/week avg burn

## FINANCIAL PROJECTIONS (Post-Cost-Cuts)
Starting cash: $38,058 at Day 98
Weekly net burn (avg after cuts): ~$3,682/week
Cash at Day 114 (R&D complete, 2.3 weeks): ~$29,600
Cash at Day 126 (4 weeks out): ~$23,300
Cash at Day 182 (12 weeks out): ~$12,000 (if recovery starts)

SURVIVAL DEPENDS ON R&D IMPROVING S1 CONVERSION TO 30%+!

## WEEK 15 PRIORITIES (URGENT)
1. 🔥 CHECK R&D status: did Tier 1 run 2 complete? (Expected Day 114)
2. 🔥 IF R&D complete: immediately ramp ads to $200/day (referral+content for S1)
3. 🔥 IF R&D complete: consider Plan C price increase to $125-$130
4. 📊 CHECK S1 conversion: should improve if R&D done
5. 📊 TRACK issues: should stay low with $450/day ops
6. 📊 MONITOR competitor events: if still silent, our quality position improves!
7. 📊 CHECK S2 Plan B retention: did the downgrades slow?

## POST-R&D PLAN (Day 114)
IF CASH > $15K AND R&D COMPLETE:
1. Ramp ads: $200/day (referral S1: $80, content S1: $60, content S2: $40, S3: $20)
2. Plan C price: $109 → $125 (quality now supports it)
3. Consider Plan B price: $55 → $65 if S2/S3 retention holds
4. Start another R&D run (Tier 1 again for +0.04 boost) if cash allows
WATCH: if S1 conversion stays below 15% after R&D → quality crisis worse than expected → emergency pivot needed

## KEY GROUP DATA (CONFIRMED)
- S1: WTP=$25.87 (±65% noise), q_floor=0.081 → actual ~0.27 (drifted), cap=272K
  - Plan A quality 0.2695 → 0.2961 after R&D → should convert 30-50%
  - Lead promo $14 (free eval at $0) — maintain this!
- S2: WTP=$178.59, q_floor=0.37→higher, cap=167K (high competitor sensitivity)
  - S2 Plan B losing subs fast (quality/price issue)
- S3: WTP=$192.98, q_floor=0.145→higher, cap=65K (high usage, high competitor sensitivity)
  - S3 Plan C: HIGH compute cost vs revenue (T4 × 570 usage) — monitor!

## QUOTA CHANGE (Week 14)
- Raised Plan A: 3000→5000 units/day
- Raised Plan B: 5000→10000 units/day
- Posted social media (Day 98): quality improvements in 2 weeks + quota raise
  - Several customers complained about rate limits in posts — social media good

## UNIT ECONOMICS (IMPORTANT - ACTUAL FROM LEDGER)
- Weekly compute: $9,477 (Day 91-98) = average 95 units/day per subscriber
- S1 Plan A: revenue $11.68/sub - compute ~$4.98 = +$6.70 margin/sub ✓ PROFITABLE
- S2 Plan B: revenue $53.44/sub - compute ~$39/sub = +$14 margin ✓ PROFITABLE
- S3 Plan C: revenue $103.15/sub - compute likely ~$100+/sub → MARGINAL or LOSS at T4!
  - KEY: average actual usage ~95 units/day (much less than estimate 570.6 which has ±65% noise)
  - If actual S3 usage is ~150-200/day: Plan C compute = 150×0.012×30 = $54/sub → losing ~$49/sub
  - If actual S3 usage ~95/day: compute = 95×0.012×30 = $34/sub → still losing at $103 revenue!
  - Wait: that means margin is positive: $103-$34 = +$69/sub! Hmm.
  - Actual compute per sub = $9,477/7/2,550 = $0.531/day = $15.93/month per sub average
  - Weighted by tier: T4 subs (131) likely $25-50/sub/month (2-3x average)
  - Bottom line: S3 Plan C is breakeven to slightly profitable; NOT the crisis I feared

## NETWORK EFFECTS (IMPORTANT)
- S3 posts → S1 reputation (0.3762) — S3 negativity SEVERELY hurts S1 acquisition
- S3 posts → S2 reputation (0.2634) — S3 issues hurt S2 acquisition
- Keep S3 quality high; resolve S3 issues fast

## MODEL TIERS COST REFERENCE
- Tier 1: $0.0003/unit (×0.60 quality) — very cheap, low quality
- Tier 2: $0.002/unit (×0.75 quality) — Plan A
- Tier 3: $0.006/unit (×0.90 quality) — Plan B (3× more expensive than T2!)
- Tier 4: $0.012/unit (×1.00 quality) — Plan C (6× more expensive than T2!)
- Tier 5: $0.030/unit (×1.10 quality) — extremely expensive

## ERRORS TO AVOID
- next-week needs 13 args: "rationale" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Subscription status is 'subscribed' not 'active' (also 'lost' for rejected leads!)
- post_social_media is nm.marketing.post_social_media() NOT analytics
- R&D list returns dict with 'tiers' key — access as projects['tiers'][index]
- R&D expected_duration_days in API response = THIS RUN's sampled duration (can differ hugely from mean!)
- set_targeted_dev_spend with empty dict {} removes ALL targeted dev spending
- Lead promo by_group REPLACES global for that group (doesn't ADD to it!)
- set_daily_spend only handles ops + dev (NOT advertising)
- Model Tier costs are expensive at T3/T4 — careful with tier changes
- DON'T raise quotas too high — heavy users exhaust quota and cost more than revenue!
- Info level 1 estimates have ±65% noise — usage estimates especially unreliable

import novamind_api as nm

# Detailed R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()

print()

# Check subscriptions breakdown
result = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as cnt, 
       AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_eff, AVG(s.listed_price) as avg_list
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status = 'subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
print('=== SUBSCRIBER BREAKDOWN ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']}: {row['cnt']} subs, eff=\${row['avg_eff']:.2f}, list=\${row['avg_list']:.2f}\")
print()

# Check promotions
promo = nm.query('''SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger 
WHERE day = 105 AND amount < 0 GROUP BY category''')
print('=== DAY 105 COSTS ===')
for row in promo['rows']:
    print(f\"  {row['category']}: \${row['total']:,.2f}\")
"

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Check recent subscriptions (last 7 days = new this week)
recent_subs = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.promotion, s.effective_price, s.start_day
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status = 'subscribed' AND s.start_day >= 99
ORDER BY s.start_day DESC LIMIT 30
''')
print('=== RECENT SUBS (Day 99+) ===')
for row in recent_subs['rows']:
    print(f\"Day {row['start_day']}: {row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']} listed=\${row['listed_price']:.2f} promo=\${row['promotion']:.2f} eff=\${row['effective_price']:.2f}\")

print()
# Check issues
issues = nm.query('''
SELECT group_id, COUNT(*) as cnt, AVG(days_open) as avg_days
FROM issues WHERE status='open'
GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY cnt DESC
''')
print('=== OPEN ISSUES BY GROUP ===')
for row in issues['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['cnt']} issues, avg {row['avg_days']:.1f} days open\")

print()
# Check current conversion rates (losses this week)
lost = nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as lost_leads
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status='lost' AND c.created_day >= 99
GROUP BY c.group_id
''')
print('=== LOST LEADS (Day 99+) ===')
for row in lost['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['lost_leads']} lost\")
"

import novamind_api as nm

# === EMERGENCY COST CUTS ===

# 1. Cut ads dramatically - S2/S3 converting 0%, save cash
# Keep minimal S1 referral+content only
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'referral_program': {'S1': 15.0},
    'content_marketing': {'S1': 5.0}
})
print('Ads cut to \$20/day (S1 only: referral \$15 + content \$5)')

# 2. Reallocate ops: cut global slightly, remove S1 targeted, ADD S3 targeted
# S3's unresolved issues (avg 2.9 days) are damaging S1 reputation (0.38 spillover!)
# Total ops: global \$350 + S3 targeted \$50 = \$400/day (save \$50/day vs \$450)
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=350, development=0)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={'S3': 50.0})
print('Ops: global \$350/day + S3 targeted \$50/day = \$400/day total (was \$450/day)')

print()
print('Savings vs previous:')
print('  Ads: \$60/day -> \$20/day = saves \$280/week')
print('  Ops: \$450/day -> \$400/day = saves \$350/week')
print('  Total: saves \$630/week')
print('  New est. weekly burn: ~\$5,150/week (vs ~\$7,300 actual this week)')
"

arguments.content:
# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 15 End (Day 105)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES ALL targeted ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- analytics.log_rationale() does NOT exist — rationale goes into next-week command
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- Lost leads DO create entries in both customers AND subscriptions (status='lost')
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name
- LEAD PROMOTIONS (set_lead_promotion): only affect EVALUATION for conversion, NOT subscription price
  - New subs subscribe at LISTED price (no promotion discount applied to billing)
  - Lead promo makes evaluation at $0 but sub pays $14 — confirmed from recent subs query

## R&D SYSTEM (CONFIRMED FROM EXPERIENCE)
- R&D returns dict with 'tiers' key: projects['tiers'][tier_index]
- Tier 1: cost=$167K, MEAN 12 days, MEAN +0.04 boost — high variance (40-50% CV)!
- Tier 2: cost=$333K, MEAN 17 days, MEAN +0.07 boost (completed, boost=0.0619)
- CURRENT R&D: Tier 1 run 2 in_progress=1 (expected completion ~Day 114±5)
  - total_quality_boost still shows 0.0289 (first run) — second run NOT YET complete
  - When complete, adds +0.0354 → new global_bonus = 0.1602 + 0.0354 = 0.1956

## CURRENT STATE (Day 105 / End of Week 15)
- Cash: $30,753 ← CRITICAL LOW
- Subscribers: 2,538 total (net -12 this week, declining)
  - S1: 1,612 Plan A ($14 new, avg eff $11.70) + 3 Plan B = 1,615 subs
  - S2: 234 Plan A + 69 Plan B + 6 Plan C = 309 subs
  - S3: 311 Plan A + 188 Plan B + 115 Plan C = 614 subs
- Open Issues: 310 (RISING from 268 last week!)
  - S1: 210 issues, avg 2.6 days
  - S3: 93 issues, avg 2.9 days (URGENT - affects S1 via reputation!)
  - S2: 53 issues, avg 2.0 days
- MONTHLY REVENUE: ~$51,752 (DECLINING ~$300-500/week from downgrades/cancellations)
- THIS WEEK NET BURN: -$7,305 (revenue $10,560 LOW week vs costs $17,866)

- All margin consumed by capacity ($6.27/sub) + ops ($5.32/sub) = net loss unless high volume

## ERRORS TO AVOID

- Subscription status is 'subscribed' not 'active' (also 'lost' for rejected leads!)
- post_social_media is nm.marketing.post_social_media() NOT analytics
- R&D list returns dict with 'tiers' key — access as projects['tiers'][index]
- set_targeted_dev_spend with empty dict {} removes ALL targeted dev spending
- Lead promo by_group REPLACES global for that group (doesn't ADD to it!)
- set_daily_spend only handles ops + dev (NOT advertising)
- DON'T raise quotas too high — heavy users exhaust quota and cost more than revenue!
- Info level 1 estimates have ±65% noise — usage estimates especially unreliable
- Capacity Tier 1 = only 200K/day — we cannot use Tier 1 with current usage (~350K/day)
- LEAD PROMOS don't affect subscription price — only help conversion evaluation
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES entire ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call

actions.result:
# CEOBench CLI Reference

## novamind-operation

Entry point for novamind-operation CLI.

Commands:
    next-week   Advance the simulation by one week (7 days).
                REQUIRES 13 args: a rationale string + for each of 4 horizons
                (+7d, +28d, +84d, +182d), submit point + 95% CI low/high.

Example:
    ./novamind-operation next-week \
        "Holding prices, raising linkedin spend on E1" \
        1050000 1000000 1100000 \
        1200000 1050000 1400000 \
        1800000 1400000 2300000 \
        3000000 2000000 4500000


### Commands

#### `./novamind-operation next-week`

Advance the simulator by one week (7 days) — REQUIRES a rationale string
plus cash predictions at four horizons, each with point estimate + 95% CI bounds.

Usage:
    novamind-operation next-week \
        "<rationale>" \
        <c1_pt> <c1_lo> <c1_hi>  \
        <c4_pt> <c4_lo> <c4_hi>  \
        <c12_pt> <c12_lo> <c12_hi>  \
        <c26_pt> <c26_lo> <c26_hi>

Arguments (all required):
    rationale         Your strategic reasoning for this week's actions
                      (non-empty quoted string). Replaces the old standalone
                      log_rationale tool. Recorded for analysis but does
                      not affect scoring.
    cash_1wk_point    Point estimate of cash 1 week from today (+7 days).
    cash_1wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for the +7-day forecast.
    cash_1wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for the +7-day forecast.
    cash_4wk_point    Point estimate of cash +28 days.
    cash_4wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for +28 days.
    cash_4wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for +28 days.
    cash_12wk_point   Point estimate of cash +84 days.
    cash_12wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +84 days.
    cash_12wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +84 days.
    cash_26wk_point   Point estimate of cash 6 months from today (+182 days).
    cash_26wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +182 days.
    cash_26wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +182 days.

Constraints (per horizon): ``lower <= point <= upper``. The server
returns 400 if violated or if any field is missing/non-numeric, or if
rationale is missing or empty.

Predictions are recorded at submission time and scored on percent error
`(point - actual) / actual` once actual cash is known. CI bounds are
scored on coverage (does the actual fall inside [lower, upper]?) and

import novamind_api as nm

# Social posts 
print('=== SOCIAL POSTS (last 14 days) ===')
posts = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=30)
if isinstance(posts, list):
    for p in posts:
        print(f"  [{p['day']}] id={p['post_id']} {p['group_id']}: {p['content'][:100]}")
elif isinstance(posts, dict):
    items = posts.get('posts', posts.get('rows', []))
    for p in items:
        print(f"  [{p.get('day','?')}] {p}")

# Enterprise turns
print('\n=== ENTERPRISE TURNS (open) ===')
ent = nm.query("SELECT customer_id, thread_type, status, message_id, day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 LIMIT 20")
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {len(ent['rows'])}")
for row in ent['rows']:
    print(f"  {row}")

# Ledger detail for last week
print('\n=== DETAILED LEDGER (last 7 days) ===')
led = nm.query("SELECT day, category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= 106 GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category")
for row in led['rows']:
    print(f"  Day {row['day']} {row['category']}: ${row['total']:.0f}")

# Check what ongoing promos are set
print('\n=== CURRENT PLAN SETTINGS ===')
print(f"Cash: ${nm.vars.current_cash:.0f}, Day: {nm.vars.current_day}")

# Check recent cancellations/downgrades
print('\n=== RECENT CHANGES (last 7 days) ===')
changes = nm.query("""SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) as cnt
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.end_day >= 106 AND s.status IN ('cancelled','downgraded')
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.status ORDER BY cnt DESC""")
for row in changes['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']} {row['status']}: {row['cnt']}")

# New subs last 7 days
new_subs = nm.query("""SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as cnt, AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.start_day >= 106 AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan""")
print('\n=== NEW SUBS (last 7 days) ===')
for row in new_subs['rows']:

"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor R&D completion and auto-ramp ads when quality improves.
Runs at the start of each week.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
print(f"R&D Tier 1: completed={tier1['completed']}, in_progress={tier1['in_progress']}, boost={tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}")

# Track quality state
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost']
s1_plan_a_quality = (0.20 + total_boost + projects['tiers'][1]['total_quality_boost']) * 0.75
# Note: need to add group bonuses — S1 has 0 group bonus currently
s1_quality_estimate = (0.20 + total_boost + 0.0619) * 0.75  # Tier1 + Tier2 boosts

print(f"Estimated S1 Plan A quality: {s1_quality_estimate:.4f} (threshold ~0.27)")

# Check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Check for enterprise turns
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")

# Decision logic based on R&D completion
# If Tier 1 run 2 has completed (boost jumped from 0.0289), ramp ads
if total_boost > 0.05:  # Second run completed (was 0.0289 from run 1, run 2 adds ~0.035)
    print("*** R&D RUN 2 COMPLETED! Ramping ads for conversion ***")
    # Ramp ads aggressively for S1 (best conversion) + S2/S3 if quality is good
    nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
        "referral_program": {"S1": 50.0, "S2": 20.0, "S3": 20.0},
        "content_marketing": {"S1": 30.0, "S2": 20.0},
        "social_media": {"S1": 20.0}
    })
    print("Ads set: referral $50/S1 $20/S2 $20/S3, content $30/S1 $20/S2, social $20/S1 = $160/day")
else:
    print("R&D Tier 1 run 2 still pending. Keeping minimal ads.")
    # Keep minimal ads
    nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})
    print("Ads: $0/day (preserving cash while quality below threshold)")

print("Auto-script complete.")

import novamind_api as nm

print(f"=== WEEK 16 ACTIONS - Day {nm.vars.current_day} ===")

# 1. Update targeted ops - add S1 ops (589 issues!) keep S3 ops for reputation
# Global $200/day + S1 targeted $50/day + S3 targeted $50/day = $300/day total
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S1": 50.0, "S3": 50.0})
print("Targeted ops set: S1=$50/day, S3=$50/day (global still $200/day = $300 total)")

# 2. Cut ads to $0 - conversion rate is ~4%, wasting cash on wasted leads
# At 4% conversion, $70/week ads + $135/week lead acquisition for only $154/month revenue
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})
print("Ads: CUT TO $0 (saving ~$205/week: $70 ads + $135 lead acquisition)")

# 3. Verify current prices/tiers/promos (no changes)

# 4. Post social media - reply to Nina Petrov's positive S3 post (228)

)

print(f"  Daily ads: $0")

# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 16 End (Day 112)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES ALL targeted ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- enterprise_turns columns: message_id, customer_id, thread_type, turn_number, sender, message_text, offer_json, day, email, seat_count, closed, close_reason (NO 'status' column!)
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- analytics.log_rationale() does NOT exist — rationale goes into next-week command
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name
- LEAD PROMOTIONS (set_lead_promotion): only affect EVALUATION for conversion, NOT subscription price
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger or market overview
- research_projects DB table has: tier, status, expected_completion_day, expected_quality_boost
- WEEKLY SCRIPTS: Put in weekly_scripts/ folder (auto-execute). register-daily-script command is BLOCKED.

## R&D SYSTEM (CONFIRMED FROM EXPERIENCE)
- R&D returns dict with 'tiers' key: projects['tiers'][tier_index]
- Tier 1: cost=$167K, MEAN 12 days, MEAN +0.04 boost — high variance (40-50% CV)!
- Tier 2: cost=$333K, MEAN 17 days, MEAN +0.07 boost (completed, boost=0.0619)

  - When complete: total_boost jumps to ~0.0643 (+0.0354 mean)
  - auto_ramp.py in weekly_scripts will detect (boost > 0.05) and auto-ramp ads

## CURRENT STATE (Day 112 / End of Week 16)
- Cash: ~$23,940 (burned $6,813 this week — WORSE than expected)
- Subscribers: 2,502 total (net -36 this week)
  - S1: 1,589 Plan A ($11.70 avg) + 1 Plan B = 1,590 subs

  - S3: 311 Plan A + 189 Plan B + 104 Plan C = 604 subs

- WEEKLY BURN EST: ~$14,250/week costs, ~$11,500/week revenue = -$2,750/week (when billing averages)

## !! CRITICAL NEW DISCOVERIES (Week 16) !!
### Competitor Events (Days 107-110): ~8 events, ~+0.085 cumulative quality drift!
- Day 107: 2 events × 0.0129 = +0.0258 to expectations
- Day 108: 2 events × 0.0117 = +0.0234
- Day 109: 2 events × 0.0089 = +0.0178

- Total: ~+0.085 quality expectation drift in ONE WEEK!

### Economic Contraction (ISM PMI 45.6-46.5):
- Posts Days 102-108: macroeconomic contraction signal

### Financial Reality:

- SPENDING MORE ON COMPUTE THAN EARNING IN REVENUE in a given week!

- Plan A unit economics: $11.70 rev - $5.70 compute = $6.00/sub, but overhead $9.35/sub = -$3.35/month!
- Plan B unit economics: $51 rev - $34.38 compute = $16.62/sub, minus overhead = +$7.27/month ✓

### Social Media Risk:
- My Day 105 post (apology) generated 6 NEGATIVE replies on Day 106 (agent post ID=16)
- Negative replies: "reactive crisis management", "hiring band-aids", "testing competitors"
- This likely DAMAGED reputation further
- Day 112: Posted POSITIVE reply to Nina Petrov S3 post (agent post ID=17)
  - "Nina, your feedback drives us... Major quality leap shipping this week"

## CASH SURVIVAL PROJECTIONS
- Cash Day 112: $23,940
- Day 119 (Week 17 end): ~$20,440 (-$3,500 avg net burn)

- Day 147 (Week 21): ~$4,440 ← NEAR ZERO if no recovery
- Day 154 (Week 22): First billing from post-R&D new subs (120+ new subs if ramp works!)
  - +120 subs × $14 = +$1,680 extra billing that week → saves us!

## POST-R&D RECOVERY PLAN (When Tier 1 run 2 completes)
### auto_ramp.py in weekly_scripts will DETECT and auto-execute when boost > 0.05:
1. Sets ads: referral $50/S1 $20/S2 $20/S3, content $30/S1 $20/S2, social $20/S1 = $160/day
2. Quality S1 Plan A: 0.2692 → ~0.2958 (significant improvement)

4. Expected S2 Plan B quality: 0.4550 → 0.4869 (borderline vs competitor-drifted threshold)

### MANUAL ACTIONS when R&D completes:

7. Consider raising Plan C price from $109 to $129 (S3 WTP=$192.98, still good margin)

## QUALITY CRISIS ANALYSIS

- S2 Plan B (T3): 0.4550 — BELOW threshold after competitor drift (0% conversion)

## KEY GROUP DATA
- S1: WTP=$25.87, Plan A quality 0.2692 (BELOW threshold, barely converting at 4%)
  - Lead promo $14 → evaluates at $0 but PAYS $14/month
  - S1 Plan A: terrible unit economics ($11.70 avg rev vs $9.35 overhead = -$3.35/month)

Key actions: (1) Cut ads to bash — 4% conversion rate makes ad spend net-negative ROI (saving ~05/week). (2) Added S1 targeted ops 0/day to address 589 S1 issues. Total ops now 00/day. (3) Replied to Nina Petrov positive S3 post (agent_post_id=17) instead of another apology — last apology post generated 6 negative replies that hurt reputation. Recovery depends entirely on R&D completing and quality improving to arrest churn. Unit economics: Plan B +/month margin, Plan A -/month, Plan C ~bash (compute nearly equals revenue). """
Weekly auto-script: Monitor R&D completion and auto-ramp ads when quality improves.
Runs at the start of each week.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
print(f"R&D Tier 1: completed={tier1['completed']}, in_progress={tier1['in_progress']}, boost={tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}")

# Track quality state
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost']
s1_plan_a_quality = (0.20 + total_boost + projects['tiers'][1]['total_quality_boost']) * 0.75
# Note: need to add group bonuses — S1 has 0 group bonus currently
s1_quality_estimate = (0.20 + total_boost + 0.0619) * 0.75  # Tier1 + Tier2 boosts

print(f"Estimated S1 Plan A quality: {s1_quality_estimate:.4f} (threshold ~0.27)")

# Check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Check for enterprise turns
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")

# Decision logic based on R&D completion
# If Tier 1 run 2 has completed (boost jumped from 0.0289), ramp ads
if total_boost > 0.05:  # Second run completed (was 0.0289 from run 1, run 2 adds ~0.035)
    print("*** R&D RUN 2 COMPLETED! Ramping ads for conversion ***")
    # Ramp ads aggressively for S1 (best conversion) + S2/S3 if quality is good
    nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
        "referral_program": {"S1": 50.0, "S2": 20.0, "S3": 20.0},
        "content_marketing": {"S1": 30.0, "S2": 20.0},
        "social_media": {"S1": 20.0}
    })
    print("Ads set: referral $50/S1 $20/S2 $20/S3, content $30/S1 $20/S2, social $20/S1 = $160/day")
else:
    print("R&D Tier 1 run 2 still pending. Keeping minimal ads.")
    # Keep minimal ads
    nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})
    print("Ads: $0/day (preserving cash while quality below threshold)")

print("Auto-script complete.")

# 1. Raise prices for new subscribers (grandfathering protects existing subs)

# 2. Ops: Reduce to save cash - global $200 + targeted S3 $30 only (S1 targeted removed)

# 3. Targeted ads - S2 and S3 (quality meets conversion thresholds)

    'social_media': {'S3': 10.0}
})
print('Ads: linkedin S2=$30 S3=$15, content S2=$20 S3=$15, social S3=$10 = $90/day total')
"

"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track competitor events, manage R&D.
Updated Week 17 - R&D Tier 1 run 2 complete, focusing on S2/S3 growth.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")
print(f"R&D in_progress: T1={tier1['in_progress']}, T2={tier2['in_progress']}")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current usage and subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
usage_result = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'", [day-7])
# Estimate daily usage from subscribers
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check enterprise turns - respond if any open
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Current: Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K/day capacity)
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K capacity) when S1 subs have churned enough
# Estimate: current usage ~ total_subs * 110 units/day
estimated_daily_usage = total_subs * 110
print(f"Estimated daily usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} units")

if estimated_daily_usage < 180000:
    print("*** USAGE BELOW THRESHOLD - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print("Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day)")
elif estimated_daily_usage < 200000:
    print("*** USAGE APPROACHING THRESHOLD - Consider Tier 1 soon ***")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} still requires Tier 2 (200K limit)")

# R&D: Start Tier 1 run 3 if cash is sufficient and not in_progress
# (Tier 1 cost ~$167K - need cash > $200K to afford safely)
if cash > 200000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print("*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 350000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print("*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} too low for R&D (need $200K+)")

# Verify ads config is maintained (safety check)
print("Ads should be: linkedin S2=$30 S3=$15, content S2=$20 S3=$15, social S3=$10 = $90/day total")
print("If not set, run: nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend({...})")

# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 17 End (Day 119)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES ALL targeted ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- enterprise_turns columns: message_id, customer_id, thread_type, turn_number, sender, message_text, offer_json, day, email, seat_count, closed, close_reason (NO 'status' column!)
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- analytics.log_rationale() does NOT exist — rationale goes into next-week command
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name
- LEAD PROMOTIONS (set_lead_promotion): only affect EVALUATION for conversion, NOT subscription price
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger or market overview
- research_projects DB table has: tier, status, expected_completion_day, expected_quality_boost
- WEEKLY SCRIPTS: Put in weekly_scripts/ folder (auto-execute). register-daily-script command is BLOCKED.

## !! CRITICAL NEW DISCOVERY: SUBSCRIPTION GRANDFATHERING !!
- Existing subscribers keep their ORIGINAL listed_price PERMANENTLY
- When I raised Plan A from $10 to $14: old $10 subs still pay $10 today (Day 119)!
- Subscription shows: 896 S1 subs at $10, 664 at $14 (same for S2/S3 with old $40/$89/$99 prices)
- Price increases ONLY affect NEW subscribers going forward
- This means: price increases are PURE UPSIDE for new subs, no churn risk from existing subs
- But churn from EXISTING subs is from QUALITY not meeting drifted expectations at their grandfathered price

## !! CRITICAL COMPETITOR DRIFT (Week 17 - Days 117-119) !!
- Day 119: QuantumEdge +0.0294, NexGen +0.0294 = +0.0588
- Day 118: CloudPeak +0.0253, QuantumEdge +0.0253 = +0.0506
- Day 117: RivalTech +0.0255 × 2 = +0.0510
- Week 17 TOTAL DRIFT: ~+0.160 quality expectation shift in ONE WEEK!
- Previous Week 16: ~+0.085
- CUMULATIVE TOTAL since start: enormous, estimated 0.40+ by Day 119
- R&D boost: +0.033 (tiny vs drift). Quality race being LOST.
- Companies posting: QuantumEdge, CloudPeak, NexGen, RivalTech, ApexSaaS (all 5 active)
- Competitor events are ACCELERATING - each week is worse than previous

## CURRENT STATE (Day 119 / End of Week 17)
- Cash: $18,787
- Subscribers: 2,461 total
  - S1: 896 at $10 + 664 at $14 = 1,560 Plan A subs
  - S2: 4 at $10 + 232 at $14 Plan A; 7 at $40 + 61 at $55 Plan B; 1 at $99 + 2 at $109 Plan C
  - S3: 74 at $10 + 232 at $14 Plan A; 51 at $40 + 148 at $55 Plan B; 10 at $89 + 42 at $99 + 37 at $109 Plan C
- Open Issues: 1,172 (crisis: 847 S1, 246 S3, 178 S2; avg days: S1=4.1, S2=5.0, S3=3.7)
- Monthly revenue potential: $48,745 ($11,368/week average)
- Weekly costs: ~$16,384 (capacity $4,240 + compute $9,597 + ops $2,350 + ads $10 + leads $187)
- Net weekly burn: ~$5,641 (CRITICAL!)

## WEEK 17 BURN ANALYSIS
- Revenue: $10,743/week (actual)
- Costs: ~$16,384/week (actual)
- Net: -$5,641/week
- Cash runway: $18,787 / $5,641 = 3.3 weeks → EMERGENCY MODE
- Outage: 90 min this week (caused issues surge from 858 to 1,172)
- New subs: only 17 (ads were $0 because auto_ramp.py missed R&D completion timing)

## WEEK 17 ACTIONS TAKEN
- Plan A raised: $14 → $20 (new subs only, grandfathered subs unaffected)
- Plan B raised: $55 → $65 (new subs only)
- Plan C: unchanged at $109
- Ops: $200/day global + S3 $30/day targeted = $230/day (save $70/day = $490/week)

## CAPACITY TIER DOWNGRADE PLAN (KEY COST SAVER!)
- Current usage: ~272K/day (Tier 2: 800K limit, Tier 1: 200K limit)
- Need to drop to <200K to use Tier 1 ($215/day vs $530/day = save $315/day = $2,205/week!)
- Threshold: when total_subs × 110 < 180,000 (conservative buffer)
- = roughly 1,636 subs needed (currently 2,461 subs)
- Need to lose ~825 more subs before can downgrade
- At 58 cancellations/week: ~14 weeks to reach threshold NATURALLY
- auto_ramp.py monitors this and will auto-downgrade

## CASH SURVIVAL PROJECTIONS (Week 18+)
- Cash Day 119: $18,787
- Day 126 (Week 18 end): ~$14,000 (-$4,400 avg burn with new ops)
- Day 133 (Week 19): ~$9,600 → DANGER
- Day 140 (Week 20): ~$5,200 → NEAR CRITICAL
- Day 147 (Week 21): ~$1,000 → NEAR ZERO
- But new S2/S3 subs from ads start billing on Day 150+ → revenue boost!
- If 10 new Plan B subs/week × 4 weeks = 40 subs × $65 = $2,600/month = $607/week extra
- This might just barely save us!

## QUALITY & CONVERSION ANALYSIS
- Global quality bonus (q_shared): 0.1919
- Total R&D boost: 0.1262 (T1=0.0643, T2=0.0619)
- S1 Plan A quality: 0.2939 (threshold at $10 eval ~0.15, $14 eval ~0.30-0.35+ after drift)
  - S1 subs at $10: MOSTLY STAYING (quality > threshold at $10)
  - S1 subs at $14: CHURNING (quality 0.2939 below their drifted expectation at $14)
  - NEW S1 leads: eval at $6 ($20-$14 promo), threshold ~0.12-0.18, quality 0.2939 → CONVERTS
- S2 Plan B quality: 0.4847 (q_min=0.37, eval $25 threshold ~0.37-0.40)
  - S2 Plan B subs at $40/$55: STAYING (quality > threshold)
  - NEW S2 leads: eval at $25 ($65-$40 promo), quality 0.4847 > threshold → CONVERTS
- S3 Plan B quality: 0.4642 (q_min=0.145, eval $35)
  - NEW S3 leads: convert to Plan B (better economics than Plan C)
- S3 Plan C quality: 0.5157 (threshold at $99-$109 range: borderline 0.48-0.55)
  - S3 Plan C subs at $89/$99: some churning (quality marginal)
  - S3 Plan C subs at $109: churning more (quality 0.5157 possibly below threshold)

## CHURN DRIVERS (58/week)
1. S1 at $14 cohort (664 subs): quality 0.2939 below drifted expectation at $14 → cancelling
2. S3 Plan C at $99-$109 (79 subs): quality 0.5157 borderline at high prices
3. Ongoing issues (1,172!) damaging satisfaction → more churn

## KEY GROUP DATA (Group Insights)
- S1: WTP=$25.87, q_min=0.081, usage=91.1/day, cap=272,812
- S2: WTP=$178.59, q_min=0.37, usage=231.5/day, cap=167,374 ← HIGH PRIORITY TARGET
- S3: WTP=$192.98, q_min=0.145, usage=570.6/day, cap=65,292

## SOCIAL MEDIA STATUS
- Day 113 post (ID=17): Replied to Nina Petrov, generated VERY POSITIVE responses from S2/S3
  - Sophie Anderson (S3): "Shipping session persistence...huge" (very positive!)
  - David Okonkwo (S2): "This is exactly what separates production-ready tools..."

- Day 119 post (ID=18): Forward-looking R&D announcement targeting Power Users
  - "R&D breakthrough #2 live. Output quality +12%... Power Users..."

## NETWORK EFFECTS

- S3 → E2 reputation (0.4413): S3 also drives enterprise E2 reputation
- S3 → E1 reputation (0.245): enterprise matters

## R&D STATUS

- TOTAL: 0.1262 boost to q_shared
- Next R&D: Need $167K minimum for Tier 1 → CANNOT AFFORD currently
- When cash > $200K: start Tier 1 run 3 (auto_ramp.py will do this)

## ERRORS TO AVOID
- next-week needs 13 args: "rationale" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Subscription status is 'subscribed' not 'active'
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger
- enterprise_turns has NO 'status' column — use closed=0 for open

## PRIORITY ACTIONS FOR WEEK 18

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Build careful cash forecast
cash = 18787.02

# Current revenue base
r = nm.query('SELECT SUM(effective_price) as monthly_rev FROM subscriptions WHERE status=\"subscribed\"')
monthly_rev = r['rows'][0]['monthly_rev']
weekly_rev = monthly_rev / 4.286
print(f'Monthly revenue: \${monthly_rev:.0f}')
print(f'Weekly revenue avg: \${weekly_rev:.0f}')

# Costs this week (actual)
actual_weekly_cost = 16384  # From actual ledger data
print(f'Actual weekly cost (last week): \${actual_weekly_cost}')

# New weekly costs (after changes)
new_capacity = 530 * 7  # $3,710/week
new_ops = 260 * 7       # $1,820/week
new_ads = 90 * 7        # $630/week
new_compute = 9300      # Estimate, declining
new_leads = 200         # Estimate with ads
new_total = new_capacity + new_ops + new_ads + new_compute + new_leads
print(f'New weekly costs estimate: \${new_total}')
print(f'  Capacity: \${new_capacity}')
print(f'  Ops: \${new_ops}')
print(f'  Ads: \${new_ads}')
print(f'  Compute: {new_compute}')
print(f'  Leads: {new_leads}')

# Churn impact on revenue (58/week current, might accelerate with drift)
# At 58 cancellations/week, avg price $15.66 = \$909/month = \$212/week revenue loss
avg_sub_price = monthly_rev / 2461  # Per month
churn_revenue_loss_weekly = 58 * avg_sub_price / 4.286  # per week
print(f'Churn revenue loss per week: \${churn_revenue_loss_weekly:.0f}')

# New subs from ads (optimistic: 20/week Plan B, billing after 30 days)
new_sub_revenue_week22 = 20 * 7 * 65 / 4.286  # 20/week * 7 weeks * $65 / billing cycle
print(f'Expected new sub revenue at Week 22: \${new_sub_revenue_week22:.0f}/week extra')

# Cash projections
print()
print('=== CASH PROJECTIONS ===')
weekly_burn_optimistic = new_total - weekly_rev  # Includes churn effects
weekly_burn = new_total - (weekly_rev - churn_revenue_loss_weekly)
weekly_burn_pessimistic = new_total - (weekly_rev - churn_revenue_loss_weekly * 1.5)  # Worse drift

def project_cash(start_cash, weeks, weekly_burn, extra_revenue_at_week=None):
    cash = start_cash
    for w in range(1, weeks+1):
        if extra_revenue_at_week and w >= extra_revenue_at_week[0]:
            burn = weekly_burn - extra_revenue_at_week[1]
        else:
            burn = weekly_burn
        cash -= burn
        print(f'  Week {18+w-1} (Day {119+w*7}): \${cash:.0f}')
    return cash

print(f'Base burn: \${weekly_burn:.0f}/week')
print()
print('Optimistic scenario (lower drift, good ad conversion):')
project_cash(cash, 8, 3800, (4, 800))
print()
print('Base scenario:')
project_cash(cash, 8, 4300, (4, 500))
print()
print('Pessimistic scenario (high drift, poor conversion):')
project_cash(cash, 8, 5200, (4, 200))
"

"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track competitor events, manage R&D.
Updated Week 17 - R&D Tier 1 run 2 complete, focusing on S2/S3 growth.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")
print(f"R&D in_progress: T1={tier1['in_progress']}, T2={tier2['in_progress']}")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current usage and subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
usage_result = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'", [day-7])
# Estimate daily usage from subscribers
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check enterprise turns - respond if any open
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Current: Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K/day capacity)
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K capacity) when S1 subs have churned enough
# Estimate: current usage ~ total_subs * 110 units/day
estimated_daily_usage = total_subs * 110
print(f"Estimated daily usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} units")

if estimated_daily_usage < 180000:
    print("*** USAGE BELOW THRESHOLD - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print("Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day)")
elif estimated_daily_usage < 200000:
    print("*** USAGE APPROACHING THRESHOLD - Consider Tier 1 soon ***")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} still requires Tier 2 (200K limit)")

# R&D: Start Tier 1 run 3 if cash is sufficient and not in_progress
# (Tier 1 cost ~$167K - need cash > $200K to afford safely)
if cash > 200000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print("*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 350000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print("*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} too low for R&D (need $200K+)")

# Verify ads config is maintained (safety check)
print("Ads should be: linkedin S2=$30 S3=$15, content S2=$20 S3=$15, social S3=$10 = $90/day total")
print("If not set, run: nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend({...})")

print("Auto-script complete.")

import novamind_api as nm

# Get current cash
r_cash = nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger')
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f'=== WEEK 18 ANALYSIS (Day {nm.vars.current_day}) ===')
print(f'Cash: \${cash:.0f}')

# Subscriber breakdown by group and price
subs = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, COUNT(*) as n, SUM(s.effective_price) as rev_30d
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = \"subscribed\"
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price
    ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price
''')
print('\\nSubs by group/plan/price:')
total_monthly_rev = 0
for r in subs['rows']:
    monthly = r['rev_30d']
    total_monthly_rev += monthly
    print(f\"  {r['group_id']} Plan {r['plan']} @\${r['listed_price']}: {r['n']} subs, ${monthly:.0f}/mo\")
print(f'TOTAL monthly rev potential: \${total_monthly_rev:.0f} = \${total_monthly_rev/4:.0f}/week')

# Check lost leads this week
lost = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status=\"lost\" AND start_day >= 119')
print(f'\\nLeads LOST (no plan suitable) since Day 119: {lost[\"rows\"][0][\"n\"]}')

# Subscribed this week  
new_subs = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status=\"subscribed\" AND start_day >= 119')
print(f'Subscribed since Day 119: {new_subs[\"rows\"][0][\"n\"]}')

# What groups are the lost leads from?
lost_by_group = nm.query('''
    SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
    WHERE s.status = \"lost\" AND s.start_day >= 119
    GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY n DESC
''')
print('Lost leads by group:')
for r in lost_by_group['rows']:
    print(f\"  {r['group_id']}: {r['n']}\")
" 2>&1

"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track R&D, manage infrastructure.
Updated Week 18 - SURVIVAL MODE. All ads cut. Focus on Tier 1 downgrade.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Estimate daily usage from recent compute costs (7-day lookback)
compute_result = nm.query(
    "SELECT SUM(ABS(amount)) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'",
    [day-7]
)
compute_7d = compute_result['rows'][0]['total'] or 0
compute_per_day = compute_7d / 7.0

# Estimated daily usage: compute_per_day / avg_model_cost_per_unit
# Current weighted avg model cost ~ $0.00433/unit (from Week 18 actuals)
# As S1 churns away and higher-tier plans dominate, this increases
# Using 0.005 as conservative estimate (biases toward higher usage estimate = safer)
avg_unit_cost = 0.005
estimated_daily_usage = compute_per_day / avg_unit_cost
print(f"Compute/day (7d avg): ${compute_per_day:.0f}, Estimated usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f}/day")

# Also check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check enterprise turns - respond if any open
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")
if open_ent > 0:
    print("!!! ENTERPRISE TURNS OPEN - CHECK IMMEDIATELY !!!")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K limit) when usage safely below limit
# Using CONSERVATIVE threshold: 170K estimated daily usage (15% buffer below 200K limit)
# This accounts for usage per sub increasing as cheap S1 subs churn away
TIER1_THRESHOLD = 170000  # 170K/day = safe buffer under 200K Tier 1 limit
TIER2_RESTORE = 185000    # Restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back up

# Check current capacity tier from recent DB state
# Assume Tier 2 unless we detect otherwise (script sets it)
current_tier2_cost = 530  # $/day for Tier 2
current_tier1_cost = 215  # $/day for Tier 1

if estimated_daily_usage < TIER1_THRESHOLD:
    print(f"*** USAGE {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} BELOW {TIER1_THRESHOLD} - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print(f"Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day) - SAVING ${current_tier2_cost - current_tier1_cost:.0f}/day!")
elif estimated_daily_usage < TIER2_RESTORE:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} approaching threshold - monitoring closely")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} still requires Tier 2 (200K limit)")

# EMERGENCY: if cash critically low, ensure Tier 1 ASAP
if cash < 8000 and total_subs < 1800:
    print(f"*** EMERGENCY CASH MODE: Forcing Tier 1! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)

# R&D: Only start if very cash-rich (need $200K+ safely above cost)
if cash > 250000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 450000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} - R&D not affordable yet (need $250K+)")

# WEEKLY BURN ESTIMATE
recent_ledger = nm.query(
    "SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? GROUP BY category",
    [day-7]
)
print("\nWeekly P&L:")
revenue = 0
costs = 0
for r in recent_ledger['rows']:
    if r['total'] > 0:
        revenue += r['total']
        print(f"  + {r['category']}: ${r['total']:.0f}")
    else:
        costs += abs(r['total'])
        print(f"  - {r['category']}: ${abs(r['total']):.0f}")
print(f"  NET: ${revenue - costs:.0f}")

print("Auto-script complete.")

# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 18 End (Day 126)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES ALL targeted ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- enterprise_turns columns: message_id, customer_id, thread_type, turn_number, sender, message_text, offer_json, day, email, seat_count, closed, close_reason (NO 'status' column!)
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- analytics.log_rationale() does NOT exist — rationale goes into next-week command
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name
- LEAD PROMOTIONS (set_lead_promotion): only affect EVALUATION for conversion, NOT subscription price
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger
- research_projects DB table has: tier, status, expected_completion_day, expected_quality_boost
- WEEKLY SCRIPTS: Put in weekly_scripts/ folder (auto-execute). register-daily-script command is BLOCKED.
- MODEL TIER COMPUTE COSTS: T1=$0.0003, T2=$0.002, T3=$0.006, T4=$0.012, T5=$0.030/unit ← HUGE DIFFERENCE!
- Upgrading Plan A T2→T5 would cost 15× more compute per usage unit = FINANCIALLY CATASTROPHIC
- Actual compute cost ~ $0.525/day/sub (avg across mix) = ~$1,145/day for 2,180 subs
- Actual daily usage per sub ≈ 121 units (from Week 18 data: 264K/day / 2,180 subs)
- set_targeted_ad_spend({}) with EMPTY DICT cuts ALL ads to zero ✓

## !! CRITICAL: NEW LEAD CONVERSION COMPLETELY BROKEN !!
- Week 18: 312 leads, only 4 subscribed, 352 LOST (no plan suitable)
- S1: 215 lost, S2: 79 lost, S3: 58 lost
- Root cause: Massive quality drift from competitor events
  - Week 18 events: +0.064+0.064+0.052+0.052+0.048+0.048+0.053+0.053+0.025+0.025 = +0.484
  - CUMULATIVE DRIFT extremely high, pushing ALL new lead quality thresholds above delivered quality
- S1 drifted q_min > 0.2904 (Plan A quality) → ALL S1 new leads fail even at $6 eval price
- S2/S3 drifted q_min also above Plan B quality (0.4805/0.4600) for new leads
- EXISTING SUBS protected by TENURE LOYALTY (+0.08-0.13 perceived quality bonus) — they stay
- New leads have NO tenure bonus → see quality below their drifted threshold → LOST FOREVER
- FIX REQUIRES R&D ($167K min) which we cannot afford currently

## !! COMPETITOR DRIFT (ACCELERATING!) !!
- Week 18: ~+0.484 quality drift (each competitor event ~0.052 average)
- Week 17: ~+0.160
- Week 16: ~+0.085
- TREND: Getting worse each week! 5 active competitors all shipping
- ALL new customer acquisition is essentially zero until R&D boosts quality

## CURRENT STATE (Day 126 / End of Week 18)
- Cash: $15,014 (down from $18,787 — burned $3,773 = $539/day)
- Subscribers: 2,180 total (down from 2,461 — lost 281 in 7 days!)
  - S1: 1,340 (779@$10 + 559@$14 + 2@$20) — churning fast
  - S2: 282 (222 Plan A, 57 Plan B, 3 Plan C)
  - S3: 558 (301 Plan A, 175 Plan B, 82 Plan C)

- S2 Plan A: 222 × $14.1 = $3,140/mo; Plan B: 57 × $53.3 = $3,040/mo; Plan C: 3 × $105.7 = $317/mo
- S3 Plan A: 301 × $13.3 = $4,016/mo; Plan B: 175 × $51.0 = $8,920/mo; Plan C: 82 × $101.8 = $8,348/mo
- TOTAL: $43,437/mo = $10,149/week

## SURVIVAL PLAN (KEY INSIGHT: 3-PHASE SURVIVAL)
### Phase 1 (Now → Week 20-21): Deep cost cuts, survive to Tier 1 downgrade

- Day 126 (now): $15,014
- Day 133 (Week 19): $15,014 - $3,012 = $11,902
- Day 140 (Week 20): $11,902 - $3,012 = $8,790
- Day 147 (Week 21): $8,790 - $3,012 = $5,778 ← CRITICAL IF NO TIER 1 YET
- Day 154 (Week 22): If Tier 1 at Day 147: $5,778 - $807 = $4,971 ← OK!
  - OR if still Tier 2: $5,778 - $3,012 = $2,766 ← DANGER
- NOTE: Revenue also declining ~15%/week as subs churn → burns slightly more

## CAPACITY DOWNGRADE TRIGGER
- auto_ramp.py now uses: compute-per-day / $0.005 < 170,000 units/day threshold
- Emergency mode: if cash < $8,000 AND subs < 1,800 → force Tier 1
- Estimated trigger: Week 20-22 depending on actual usage mix

## CURRENT CONFIGURATION (Week 19 start)
- Plan A: Tier 2 (×0.75), $20, 5,000/day quota
- Plan B: Tier 3 (×0.90), $65, 50,000/day quota (INCREASED from 10K — fixing rate limit complaints)
- Plan C: Tier 4 (×1.00), $109, 100,000/day quota (INCREASED from 20K — fixing rate limit complaints)

- Dev: $0/day
- Lead promotions: global=$5, S1=$14, S2=$40, S3=$30 (still set but irrelevant for conversions)

## DELIVERED QUALITY (Day 126)
- Global bonus (q_shared): 0.1873 (DOWN from 0.1919 — quality declining without dev spend!)
- Total R&D boost: 0.1262 (T1=0.0643, T2=0.0619) included in q_shared
- S1 Plan A: 0.2904 (TOO LOW for new leads given drift)
- S2 Plan A: 0.4004, Plan B: 0.4805, Plan C: 0.5339 (group bonus +0.1466)
- S3 Plan A: 0.3833, Plan B: 0.4600, Plan C: 0.5111 (group bonus +0.1238)
- CRITICAL: q_shared is DECLINING (from 0.1919 → 0.1873 in 7 days). Need dev spend to halt this OR it's natural decay. Monitor!

## SOCIAL MEDIA STATUS
- Day 119 post (ID=18): R&D announcement — generated positive S2, S3, E3 responses!
  - Day 120: E3 said "let's sync on enterprise terms" (expressed interest but no turn arrived)
  - Day 120: S2, S3 confirmed quality improvement legit
- Day 126 post (ID=19): Quota increase announcement (Plan B 50K, Plan C 100K)
  - Addresses rate limit complaints visible in social feed
- Key negative posts: users hitting rate limits (addressed by quota increase)
- Key positive: S2/S3 quality appreciation from Day 119 R&D announcement

## CHURN ANALYSIS (283 cancellations in Week 18)
- PRIMARY DRIVER: Quality drift — new lead q_min has drifted above delivered quality
- S1 main churner: 1,340 → will churn ~200/week until mostly gone by Week 22-24

- But issues (901 S1, 134 S2, 228 S3) also driving satisfaction-based churn

## LONG-TERM STEADY STATE (once S1 mostly churned ~Week 24)

- Quality for enterprise Plan C = 0.3873 (too low for most enterprise needs given drift)
- To attract enterprise: need quality boost from R&D first
- If enterprise turns arrive: respond within 7 days (1 week) or PERMANENTLY LOSE lead!

## ERRORS TO AVOID
- next-week needs 13 args: "rationale" (quoted) + 12 numbers

## PRIORITY ACTIONS FOR WEEK 19

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# Detailed weekly model for forecasting
# Week 18 actuals (8 days): revenue +12351, compute -9161, capacity -4240, ops -2120, ads -630, leads -196
# Weekly (7-day) equiv: revenue=10807, compute=8016, capacity=3710, ops=1855, ads=551, leads=171
# NET WEEK 18: 10807 - 8016 - 3710 - 1855 - 551 - 171 = -3496 ≈ -3773 (slight diff=rounding+8d period)

# Week 19+ changes:
# 1. No ads: save 551/wk
# 2. Lead cost: only organic ~50 leads = 25/wk (save 146/wk)
# 3. Compute: declining as subs churn (-281/wk net loss)
#    Compute per sub per week = 8016/2180 = 3.676/sub/wk
#    Week 19: (2180-281) subs → compute = 1899*3.676 = 6,981/wk
# 4. Revenue declining: 10807 * (1899/2180) = 9,407/wk

# But also: as S1 churns faster and S2/S3 stay, compute per sub increases
# (S3/S2 are heavier users on higher tier plans = more expensive)
# Conservative: add 5% compute cost premium for mix shift

wk18_compute_per_sub = 8016/2180  # $3.676/sub/wk

def project_week(subs, prev_cash, is_tier1=False):
    s1_frac = max(0, (subs-840)/subs) if subs > 840 else 0
    s23_frac = 1 - s1_frac
    # Compute cost per sub (higher for S2/S3 mix)
    compute_per_sub = wk18_compute_per_sub * (1 + 0.3 * s23_frac)
    compute = subs * compute_per_sub
    capacity = 1505 if is_tier1 else 3710
    ops = 1855
    leads = 25
    
    # Revenue: monthly billing rate (7/30 of monthly subs)
    # S1 avg $11.7, S2 avg $23, S3 avg $34.7
    s1_count = max(0, subs - 840)
    s23_count = min(840, subs)  
    s2_count = s23_count * 282/840
    s3_count = s23_count * 558/840
    monthly_rev = s1_count*11.7 + s2_count*23 + s3_count*34.7
    weekly_rev = monthly_rev * 7/30
    
    costs = compute + capacity + ops + leads
    net = weekly_rev - costs
    return prev_cash + net, weekly_rev, costs, net

# Simulations
print('=== CASH PROJECTIONS ===')
cash = 15014
subs = 2180
churn_rates = {'s1': 220, 's23': 61}  # per week
tier1_threshold = 1417  # subs (from auto_ramp)
is_tier1 = False

print(f'Day 126 (W18 end): Cash=${cash:.0f}, Subs={subs}')
for week in range(1, 13):
    # Apply churn
    s1_churn = min(max(0, subs-840), churn_rates['s1'])
    s23_churn = min(840, subs) * 0.072  # 7.2%/week
    net_churn = s1_churn + s23_churn
    new_subs = max(840, subs - net_churn)
    
    # Tier 1 check
    if new_subs < tier1_threshold and not is_tier1:
        print(f'  *** TIER 1 DOWNGRADE at Week {18+week}! ***')
        is_tier1 = True
    
    new_cash, rev, costs, net = project_week(new_subs, cash, is_tier1)
    
    if week in [1, 4, 12]:
        print(f'Week {18+week} (Day {126+week*7}): Cash=${new_cash:.0f}, Subs={new_subs:.0f}, Rev/wk=${rev:.0f}, Costs/wk=${costs:.0f}, Net=${net:.0f}, Tier1={is_tier1}')
    
    cash = new_cash
    subs = new_subs

print()
print('26-week projection:')
# Continue for 26 weeks total
cash = 15014
subs = 2180
is_tier1 = False
for week in range(1, 27):
    s1_churn = min(max(0, subs-840), churn_rates['s1'])
    s23_churn = min(840, subs) * 0.072
    net_churn = s1_churn + s23_churn
    new_subs = max(840, subs - net_churn)
    if new_subs < tier1_threshold and not is_tier1:
        is_tier1 = True
    new_cash, rev, costs, net = project_week(new_subs, cash, is_tier1)
    if week == 26:
        print(f'Week {18+26} (Day {126+26*7}): Cash=${new_cash:.0f}, Subs={new_subs:.0f}')
    cash = new_cash
    subs = new_subs
" 2>&1

actions.result:
"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track R&D, manage infrastructure.
Updated Week 18 - SURVIVAL MODE. All ads cut. Focus on Tier 1 downgrade.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Estimate daily usage from recent compute costs (7-day lookback)
compute_result = nm.query(
    "SELECT SUM(ABS(amount)) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'",
    [day-7]
)
compute_7d = compute_result['rows'][0]['total'] or 0
compute_per_day = compute_7d / 7.0

# Estimated daily usage: compute_per_day / avg_model_cost_per_unit
# Current weighted avg model cost ~ $0.00433/unit (from Week 18 actuals)
# As S1 churns away and higher-tier plans dominate, this increases
# Using 0.005 as conservative estimate (biases toward higher usage estimate = safer)
avg_unit_cost = 0.005
estimated_daily_usage = compute_per_day / avg_unit_cost
print(f"Compute/day (7d avg): ${compute_per_day:.0f}, Estimated usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f}/day")

# Also check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check enterprise turns - respond if any open
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")
if open_ent > 0:
    print("!!! ENTERPRISE TURNS OPEN - CHECK IMMEDIATELY !!!")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K limit) when usage safely below limit
# Using CONSERVATIVE threshold: 170K estimated daily usage (15% buffer below 200K limit)
# This accounts for usage per sub increasing as cheap S1 subs churn away
TIER1_THRESHOLD = 170000  # 170K/day = safe buffer under 200K Tier 1 limit
TIER2_RESTORE = 185000    # Restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back up

# Check current capacity tier from recent DB state
# Assume Tier 2 unless we detect otherwise (script sets it)
current_tier2_cost = 530  # $/day for Tier 2
current_tier1_cost = 215  # $/day for Tier 1

if estimated_daily_usage < TIER1_THRESHOLD:
    print(f"*** USAGE {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} BELOW {TIER1_THRESHOLD} - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print(f"Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day) - SAVING ${current_tier2_cost - current_tier1_cost:.0f}/day!")
elif estimated_daily_usage < TIER2_RESTORE:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} approaching threshold - monitoring closely")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} still requires Tier 2 (200K limit)")

# EMERGENCY: if cash critically low, ensure Tier 1 ASAP
if cash < 8000 and total_subs < 1800:
    print(f"*** EMERGENCY CASH MODE: Forcing Tier 1! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)

# R&D: Only start if very cash-rich (need $200K+ safely above cost)
if cash > 250000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 450000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} - R&D not affordable yet (need $250K+)")

# WEEKLY BURN ESTIMATE
recent_ledger = nm.query(
    "SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? GROUP BY category",
    [day-7]
)
print("\nWeekly P&L:")
revenue = 0
costs = 0
for r in recent_ledger['rows']:
    if r['total'] > 0:
        revenue += r['total']
        print(f"  + {r['category']}: ${r['total']:.0f}")
    else:
        costs += abs(r['total'])
        print(f"  - {r['category']}: ${abs(r['total']):.0f}")
print(f"  NET: ${revenue - costs:.0f}")

print("Auto-script complete.")

import novamind_api as nm

# Subscription breakdown by group and plan
r = nm.query('''
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price, AVG(s.seat_count) as avg_seats
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
  WHERE s.status='subscribed'
  GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
''')
print('=== SUBSCRIBER BREAKDOWN ===')
total_subs = 0
total_monthly = 0
for row in r['rows']:
    monthly = row['n'] * row['avg_price']
    total_subs += row['n']
    total_monthly += monthly
    print(f\"{row['group_id']} Plan {row['plan']}: {row['n']} subs @ avg \${row['avg_price']:.2f} = \${monthly:.0f}/mo\")
print(f'TOTAL: {total_subs} subs, \${total_monthly:.0f}/month = \${total_monthly/30*7:.0f}/week')

# Issues by group
r2 = nm.query('SELECT group_id, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(days_open) as avg_days FROM issues WHERE status=\"open\" GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY n DESC')
print()
print('=== OPEN ISSUES ===')
for row in r2['rows']:
    print(f\"{row['group_id']}: {row['n']} issues, avg {row['avg_days']:.1f} days open\")

# Enterprise turns
r3 = nm.query('SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 LIMIT 10')
print()
print(f'=== ENTERPRISE TURNS (open): {len(r3[\"rows\"])} ===')
for row in r3['rows']:
    print(f\"  Customer {row['customer_id']}: {row['thread_type']}, turn {row['turn_number']}, day {row['day']}\")
"

next-week   Advance the simulation by one week (7 days).
                REQUIRES 13 args: a rationale string + for each of 4 horizons
                (+7d, +28d, +84d, +182d), submit point + 95% CI low/high.

Example:
    ./novamind-operation next-week \
        "Holding prices, raising linkedin spend on E1" \
        1050000 1000000 1100000 \
        1200000 1050000 1400000 \
        1800000 1400000 2300000 \
        3000000 2000000 4500000
--
#### `./novamind-operation next-week`

Advance the simulator by one week (7 days) — REQUIRES a rationale string
plus cash predictions at four horizons, each with point estimate + 95% CI bounds.

Usage:
    novamind-operation next-week \
        "<rationale>" \
        <c1_pt> <c1_lo> <c1_hi>  \
        <c4_pt> <c4_lo> <c4_hi>  \
        <c12_pt> <c12_lo> <c12_hi>  \
        <c26_pt> <c26_lo> <c26_hi>

./novamind-operation python-c "
import novamind_api as nm

# KEY COST ANALYSIS
# Total usage this week: 1,901,070 units
# Current model tiers: A=T2(\$0.002/unit), B=T3(\$0.006/unit), C=T4(\$0.012/unit)
# After downgrade: A=T2, B=T2, C=T3

# Current sub counts:
# Plan A (T2): S1 1143 + S2 217 + S3 287 = 1647 subs
# Plan B (T3): S2 46 + S3 171 = 217 subs  
# Plan C (T4): S2 4 + S3 73 = 77 subs

# Compute savings if B->T2 and C->T3:
# Plan B: 217 subs, avg 450 units/day, T3->T2: saves $0.004/unit
# Plan C: 77 subs, avg 450 units/day, T4->T3: saves $0.006/unit  
weekly_usage_planB = 217 * 450 * 7  # 683,550 units/week
weekly_usage_planC = 77 * 450 * 7   # 242,550 units/week
savings_B = weekly_usage_planB * 0.004  # \$2,734/week
savings_C = weekly_usage_planC * 0.006  # \$1,455/week
print(f'Plan B->T2 weekly compute savings: \${savings_B:.0f}')
print(f'Plan C->T3 weekly compute savings: \${savings_C:.0f}')
print(f'Total weekly compute savings: \${savings_B+savings_C:.0f}')
print(f'Daily compute savings: \${(savings_B+savings_C)/7:.0f}')

# Execute tier downgrade
print()
print('=== EXECUTING MODEL TIER DOWNGRADE ===')
nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=2, C=3)
print('Model tiers set: A=T2, B=T2, C=T3')

# Cut ops spending: redirect from global to targeted S2/S3
print()
print('=== ADJUSTING OPS SPEND ===')
# Global ops: $200->$50/day (S1 issues can fester, accelerating their churn)
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=50, development=0)
# Targeted ops: S2->$75/day, S3->$75/day (protect high-value subs) 
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend({'S2': 75, 'S3': 75})
print('Global ops: \$50/day, S2 targeted: \$75/day, S3 targeted: \$75/day = \$200/day total')
print(f'Weekly ops savings: \$420')

print()
print('New estimated daily costs:')
compute_new = (1901070/7) * 0.00255  # new weighted avg
print(f'  Compute (est): \${compute_new:.0f}/day')
print(f'  Capacity (T2): \$530/day')
print(f'  Ops: \$200/day')
print(f'  Leads: ~\$14/day')
print(f'  TOTAL: ~\${compute_new+530+200+14:.0f}/day')
print(f'  vs PREVIOUS: ~\${1344+530+260+14:.0f}/day')
print(f'  SAVINGS: ~\${(1344+530+260+14)-(compute_new+530+200+14):.0f}/day = \${7*((1344+530+260+14)-(compute_new+530+200+14)):.0f}/week')
"
