arguments.content:
"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track R&D, manage infrastructure.
Updated Week 19 - EMERGENCY COST CUTS. Model tiers downgraded B->T2, C->T3.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Estimate daily usage from recent compute costs (7-day lookback)
compute_result = nm.query(
    "SELECT SUM(ABS(amount)) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'",
    [day-7]
)
compute_7d = compute_result['rows'][0]['total'] or 0
compute_per_day = compute_7d / 7.0

# UPDATED: After model tier downgrade (B->T2, C->T3):
# New weighted avg compute cost ~ $0.0025/unit (was $0.005 with old T3/T4)
# This is more accurate post-downgrade
avg_unit_cost = 0.0025
estimated_daily_usage = compute_per_day / avg_unit_cost
print(f"Compute/day (7d avg): ${compute_per_day:.0f}, Estimated usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f}/day")

# Also check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check enterprise turns - respond if any open
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")
if open_ent > 0:
    print("!!! ENTERPRISE TURNS OPEN - CHECK IMMEDIATELY !!!")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K limit) when usage safely below limit
# UPDATED thresholds: 
# - TIER1_THRESHOLD: 170K/day (15% buffer below 200K Tier 1 limit)
# - TIER2_RESTORE: 185K/day (restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back)
TIER1_THRESHOLD = 170000  # 170K/day = safe buffer under 200K Tier 1 limit
TIER2_RESTORE = 185000    # Restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back up

# Check current capacity tier from recent DB state
current_tier2_cost = 530  # $/day for Tier 2
current_tier1_cost = 215  # $/day for Tier 1

if estimated_daily_usage < TIER1_THRESHOLD:
    print(f"*** USAGE {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} BELOW {TIER1_THRESHOLD} - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print(f"Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day) - SAVING ${current_tier2_cost - current_tier1_cost:.0f}/day!")
elif estimated_daily_usage < TIER2_RESTORE:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} approaching threshold - monitoring closely")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} still requires Tier 2 (800K limit)")

# EMERGENCY: if cash critically low, force review
if cash < 5000:
    print(f"*** EMERGENCY CASH MODE: Cash=${cash:.0f} — consider Tier 1 or further ops cuts ***")
    # Don't force Tier 1 automatically since we may exceed capacity
    
if cash < 3000:
    print(f"*** CRITICAL: Cash < $3000 — MUST cut ops to survive ***")
    nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=30, development=0)
    nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend({'S2': 50, 'S3': 50})

# R&D: Only start if very cash-rich (need $200K+ safely above cost)
if cash > 250000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 450000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} - R&D not affordable yet (need $250K+)")

# WEEKLY BURN ESTIMATE
recent_ledger = nm.query(
    "SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? GROUP BY category",
    [day-7]
)
print("\nWeekly P&L:")
revenue = 0
costs = 0
for r in recent_ledger['rows']:
    if r['total'] > 0:
        revenue += r['total']
        print(f"  + {r['category']}: ${r['total']:.0f}")
    else:
        costs += abs(r['total'])
        print(f"  - {r['category']}: ${abs(r['total']):.0f}")
print(f"  NET: ${revenue - costs:.0f}")

print("Auto-script complete.")

arguments.content:
# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 19 End (Day 133)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- analytics module has NO log_rationale() function (confirmed from docs)
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES ALL targeted ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- enterprise_turns columns: message_id, customer_id, thread_type, turn_number, sender, message_text, offer_json, day, email, seat_count, closed, close_reason (NO 'status' column!)
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name
- LEAD PROMOTIONS (set_lead_promotion): only affect EVALUATION for conversion, NOT subscription price
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger
- research_projects DB table has: tier, status, expected_completion_day, expected_quality_boost
- WEEKLY SCRIPTS: Put in weekly_scripts/ folder (auto-execute). register-daily-script command is BLOCKED.
- MODEL TIER COMPUTE COSTS: T1=$0.0003, T2=$0.002, T3=$0.006, T4=$0.012, T5=$0.030/unit ← HUGE DIFFERENCE!
- set_targeted_ad_spend({}) with EMPTY DICT cuts ALL ads to zero ✓

## CRITICAL COST DISCOVERY (Week 19)
- COMPUTE was killing us: Plan B (T3=$0.006/unit) and Plan C (T4=$0.012/unit) were LOSING MONEY
  - Plan B T3: 217 subs × 450 units/day × $0.006 = $585/day compute vs $502/day revenue → NEGATIVE MARGIN!
  - Plan C T4: 77 subs × 450 units/day × $0.012 = $415/day compute vs $309/day revenue → NEGATIVE MARGIN!
- Week 19 actual: $14,047 in costs vs $7,890 in revenue = -$6,157 net (worse than expected!)
  - Compute alone: $9,407/week (the culprit)
- FIXED: Downgraded Plan B T3→T2 and Plan C T4→T3 on Day 133
  - Saves ~$670-711/day = $4,690-4,980/week in compute costs!
  - New weighted avg compute cost: ~$0.0025/unit (was $0.005/unit)

## CURRENT STATE (Day 133 / End of Week 19)
- Cash: $8,858 (dangerously low but tier downgrade just executed)
- Subscribers: 1,941 total (down from 2,180 — losing ~240/week)
  - S1 Plan A: 1,143 @ avg $11.59 = $13,246/mo (churning ~200/week!)
  - S2 Plan A: 217 @ $14.34, Plan B: 46 @ $52.93, Plan C: 4 @ $106.50 = $5,973/mo
  - S3 Plan A: 287 @ $13.48, Plan B: 171 @ $51.40, Plan C: 73 @ $102.29 = $20,127/mo
  - TOTAL: $39,346/month = $9,181/week (EXPECTED avg; actual billing is lumpy)
- Open Issues: 674 (S1: 860, S3: 215, S2: 88)
- New subs this week: 0 (continued total conversion failure)
- Cancellations: 239 (S1: 197, S3: 27, S2: 12, Plan B/C: only 9 total)

## CURRENT CONFIGURATION (Week 20 start)
- Plan A: Tier 2 (×0.75), $20, 5,000/day quota
- Plan B: Tier 2 (×0.75), $65, 50,000/day quota ← DOWNGRADED from T3 to save compute!
- Plan C: Tier 3 (×0.90), $109, 100,000/day quota ← DOWNGRADED from T4 to save compute!
- Capacity: Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K/day)
- Global Ops: $50/day, S2 Targeted: $75/day, S3 Targeted: $75/day = $200/day total
- Ads: ALL $0 (quality gap makes all ads 0% ROI)
- Dev: $0/day

## DELIVERED QUALITY (Day 133) — post-tier-downgrade
- q_shared: 0.1842 (declining ~-0.003/week without dev spend)
- Plan A (T2): (0.20 + 0.1842) × 0.75 = 0.2881 for S1/E groups (no bonus)
- Plan B (now T2! same as A): S2: (0.20 + 0.1842 + 0.1466) × 0.75 = 0.3981
- Plan C (now T3): S2: (0.20 + 0.1842 + 0.1466) × 0.90 = 0.4777 (was 0.5308 at T4)
- S3 Plan B T2: (0.20 + 0.1842 + 0.1238) × 0.75 = 0.3810
- S3 Plan C T3: (0.20 + 0.1842 + 0.1238) × 0.90 = 0.4572 (was 0.5080 at T4)
- NOTE: Plan B now has SAME quality as Plan A — differentiation is quota only!
  - Risk: S2/S3 Plan B subs at $65 may churn when they notice quality = Plan A ($20)
  - BUT: Existing subs have tenure loyalty (+0.08-0.13 perceived bonus) — watch for churn!

## NEW FINANCIAL MODEL (Post-Tier-Downgrade)
- New weighted avg compute cost: ~$0.0025/unit (down from $0.005)
- Estimated new daily compute: ~$650-693/day (down from $1,344/day)
- Daily costs: $693 compute + $530 capacity + $200 ops + $14 leads = $1,437/day
- Daily revenue (monthly avg): $39,346/30 = $1,311/day
- Net: -$126/day = -$882/week ← MUCH BETTER than -$1,023/day before!
- Cash runway: $8,858 / $126 = ~70 days (up from 8.7 days!)
- BUT: Revenue is declining ~$144/day per week as subs churn!

## CASH SURVIVAL PROJECTIONS (Post Tier-Downgrade)
- Week 20 (Day 140): $8,858 - ~$1,500 = ~$7,358
- Week 21 (Day 147): ~$7,358 - ~$2,000 = ~$5,358
- Week 22 (Day 154): ~$5,358 - ~$2,500 = ~$2,858 ← CRITICAL
- Week 23 (Day 161): IF Tier 1 triggers (saves $2,205/week): $2,858 - $500 = ~$2,358 (survivable!)
  OR if no Tier 1: $2,858 - $3,000 = -$142 → BANKRUPT
- Key: MUST get to Tier 1 by Week 22-23

## TIER 1 DOWNGRADE TRIGGER (Updated)
- Tier 1 limit: 200K units/day; threshold: 170K/day (buffer)
- Current usage: 271,581/day (too high for Tier 1!)
- After tier downgrade + S1 churn:
  - When S1 drops from 1,143 to ~200: S1 T2 usage: 200×75=15K/day
  - S2/S3 Plan A T2: 504×150=75.6K/day, Plan B T2: 217×450=97.7K/day, Plan C T3: 77×450=34.7K/day
  - At S1=200: total = 15K+75.6K+97.7K+34.7K = 223K/day → STILL over 200K!
  - Need S2/S3 Plan B usage to drop OR some Plan B/C churn from tier downgrade
  - If 50 Plan B churn: 167×450=75.2K/day → total with S1=200: 15K+75.6K+75.2K+34.7K=200.5K ← borderline
  - Realistic Tier 1 trigger: Week 22-24 as S1 churns + some Plan B/C churn from quality drop
- auto_ramp.py: uses avg_unit_cost=$0.0025 to estimate usage from compute costs

## Q_SHARED DECLINE (ONGOING THREAT)
- Week 17: 0.1919 → Week 18: 0.1873 → Week 19: 0.1842
- Declining ~0.003/week without dev spend
- In 10 weeks: 0.1842 - 0.030 = 0.1542
- S2 Plan B quality in 10 weeks (T2): (0.20+0.1542+0.1466)×0.75 = 0.3756 (vs today 0.3981)
  - With tenure +0.10: 0.4756 perceived — still acceptable at $52/month
- S2 Plan C quality in 10 weeks (T3): (0.20+0.1542+0.1466)×0.90 = 0.4507 (vs today 0.4777)
  - With tenure +0.10: 0.5507 perceived — OK
- Plan C subs most resilient; Plan B subs at risk in 10+ weeks
- Cannot afford dev spend to halt this (~$500/day needed to stabilize)

## SOCIAL MEDIA SIGNALS (Day 133)
- NEGATIVE: Multiple posts about unresolved support tickets (2+ weeks with no response!)
  - "ghosted me for 3+ days on support ticket" — hurting reputation
  - "bug report 2 weeks ago... crickets" — severe issue backlog perception
- COMPETITOR: NexGen +0.009 quality bump; CloudPeak +0.009 quality bump (Day 132-133)
  - Continued competitor drift on customer quality expectations
- MACRO: ISM PMI at 44.2 — enterprise budget freezes! Enterprise leads unlikely near-term
- DAY 127 POSITIVE: E1 and S2/S3 happy about quota increases (those subs staying)

## CHURN ANALYSIS (Week 19)
- S1: 197/week (almost entirely Plan A) — EXPECTED, quality drift + 860 open issues at 10.5d
- S2/S3 Plan A: 31/week (S3:19, S2:12) — CONCERNING! May be driven by issues (88 S2, 215 S3)
- S2/S3 Plan B/C: only 9/week total from 294 subs = 3% monthly — HEALTHY!
- After tier downgrade (B→T2): Risk of Plan B churn from quality drop (0.4572→0.3810 for S3)
  - Tenure loyalty should buffer: perceived 0.3810+0.10=0.4810 — probably OK at $51/month
  - Monitor Week 20 for elevated Plan B/C churn

## LONG-TERM STEADY STATE (Weeks 22-26)
- S1 fully churned by Week 23-24 (~200/week × 6 weeks)
- Remaining: ~500-600 S2/S3 subs at avg $57-62/month
- Revenue: $28,500-37,200/month = $1,000-1,240/day
- Compute (T2/T3 for S2/S3): ~$480-554/day
- Tier 1 ($215/day) feasible when S2/S3 usage < 200K/day
- Ops: $200/day
- Net: $1,000 - ($480+$215+$200) = +$105/day → BARELY PROFITABLE!
- Path to improvement: R&D when cash > $250K, then re-start conversions for S2/S3

## R&D STATUS
- Tier 1: completed=2, total_boost=0.0643 — need run 3 when cash > $250K
- Tier 2: completed=1, total_boost=0.0619
- Tier 3: $500K cost (way out of reach)
- Plan: Start Tier 1 run 3 when cash > $250K (auto_ramp.py handles this)

## ENTERPRISE STATUS
- Currently: 0 enterprise subscribers, 0 open turns
- Enterprise quality (Plan C T3): 0.4572 — may be too low for enterprise needs
- Macro headwinds: ISM PMI 44.2 → enterprise budget freezes → reduced enterprise leads
- If enterprise turns arrive: respond within 7 days (1 week) or PERMANENTLY LOSE lead!

## ERRORS TO AVOID
- next-week needs 13 args: "rationale" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Subscription status is 'subscribed' not 'active'
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger
- enterprise_turns has NO 'status' column — use closed=0 for open
- post_social_media is nm.marketing.post_social_media() NOT analytics
- R&D list returns dict with 'tiers' key
- set_targeted_dev_spend with empty dict {} removes ALL targeted dev spending
- set_targeted_ops_spend REPLACES entire ops config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- Social media apology posts BACKFIRE — reply to POSITIVE posts or post forward-looking content
- WEEKLY SCRIPTS auto-execute from weekly_scripts/ folder at start of each week
- get_social_posts() returns dict with 'posts' key, not a list!
- Lead promo by_group REPLACES global for that group (doesn't ADD to it!)
- GRANDFATHERING: existing subs keep original price. Price increases only affect NEW subs!
- MODEL TIER UPGRADES ARE VERY EXPENSIVE: T5 costs 15× T2 in compute! Don't upgrade!
- set_targeted_ad_spend({}) with empty dict = cuts all ads ✓ (confirmed working)
- Issues query: use GROUP BY group_id to get issues per group
- DO NOT post social media about service quality issues or downgrades — draws attention to negatives
- PLAN B IS NOW SAME QUALITY AS PLAN A (both T2) — differentiation only by quota

## PRIORITY ACTIONS FOR WEEK 20
1. CHECK Plan B/C churn — if elevated (>20 Plan B/C cancellations/week), may need to consider T2→T3 for Plan B
2. Check if auto_ramp.py correctly estimates usage with new $0.0025 avg cost
3. Watch for Tier 1 downgrade trigger (need usage < 170K/day est. from compute)
4. Monitor q_shared — declining at ~0.003/week; if accelerates, watch existing S2/S3 churn
5. ENTERPRISE TURNS: respond within 7 days if any arrive!
6. Consider social media post to address support complaints (careful not to backfire)
7. Check if total ops ($200) is actually resolving S2/S3 issues faster

import novamind_api as nm

# Verify current config

actions.result:
# CEOBench CLI Reference

## novamind-operation

Entry point for novamind-operation CLI.

Commands:
    next-week   Advance the simulation by one week (7 days).
                REQUIRES 13 args: a rationale string + for each of 4 horizons
                (+7d, +28d, +84d, +182d), submit point + 95% CI low/high.

Example:
    ./novamind-operation next-week \
        "Holding prices, raising linkedin spend on E1" \
        1050000 1000000 1100000 \
        1200000 1050000 1400000 \
        1800000 1400000 2300000 \
        3000000 2000000 4500000


### Commands

#### `./novamind-operation next-week`

Advance the simulator by one week (7 days) — REQUIRES a rationale string
plus cash predictions at four horizons, each with point estimate + 95% CI bounds.

Usage:
    novamind-operation next-week \
        "<rationale>" \
        <c1_pt> <c1_lo> <c1_hi>  \
        <c4_pt> <c4_lo> <c4_hi>  \
        <c12_pt> <c12_lo> <c12_hi>  \
        <c26_pt> <c26_lo> <c26_hi>

Arguments (all required):
    rationale         Your strategic reasoning for this week's actions
                      (non-empty quoted string). Replaces the old standalone
                      log_rationale tool. Recorded for analysis but does
                      not affect scoring.
    cash_1wk_point    Point estimate of cash 1 week from today (+7 days).
    cash_1wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for the +7-day forecast.
    cash_1wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for the +7-day forecast.
    cash_4wk_point    Point estimate of cash +28 days.
    cash_4wk_lower    95% CI lower bound for +28 days.
    cash_4wk_upper    95% CI upper bound for +28 days.
    cash_12wk_point   Point estimate of cash +84 days.
    cash_12wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +84 days.
    cash_12wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +84 days.
    cash_26wk_point   Point estimate of cash 6 months from today (+182 days).
    cash_26wk_lower   95% CI lower bound for +182 days.
    cash_26wk_upper   95% CI upper bound for +182 days.

Constraints (per horizon): ``lower <= point <= upper``. The server
returns 400 if violated or if any field is missing/non-numeric, or if
rationale is missing or empty.

Predictions are recorded at submission time and scored on percent error
`(point - actual) / actual` once actual cash is known. CI bounds are
scored on coverage (does the actual fall inside [lower, upper]?) and
sharpness (interval width / actual). You are evaluated on prediction
accuracy + calibration at each horizon in addition to realized cash.

Calls the API server to step the simulation forward by one week.
Prints the dashboard to stdout, which includes key metrics,
this week's results, and inbox notifications.

**NOTE:** The next_week call may take several minutes at large subscriber
counts. This is normal — just wait for the response.

Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure (including missing rationale or predictions).

## novamind

Entry point for novamind CLI.

Commands:
    register-daily-script   Register a script to run daily
    list-daily-scripts      List all registered daily scripts
    remove-daily-script     Remove a registered daily script

Examples:
    novamind register-daily-script strategy.py
    novamind list-daily-scripts
    novamind remove-daily-script strategy.py


### Commands

#### `novamind register-daily-script`

Register a Python script to run automatically at the start of each day.

The script content is snapshotted at registration time. Subsequent edits
to the source file will NOT affect the registered version. To update,
re-register the script.

If a script with the same filename already exists, it is overwritten.
Scripts are executed in alphabetical order at the start of each day,
with novamind_api pre-imported.

Args:
    script_path: Path to the Python script to register.

Example:
    novamind register-daily-script my_strategy.py

#### `novamind list-daily-scripts`

List all registered daily scripts.

Shows script names and sizes. Scripts run at the start of each day
in alphabetical order.

Example:
    novamind list-daily-scripts

#### `novamind remove-daily-script`

Remove a registered daily script.

Args:
    script_name: Filename of the script to remove.

Example:
    novamind remove-daily-script my_strategy.py

import novamind_api as nm

# Billing calendar: next 14 days
today = 140
upcoming_billing = nm.query('''
SELECT billing_day_mod30, COUNT(*) as n, SUM(effective_price * seat_count) as revenue
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status = 'subscribed'
GROUP BY billing_day_mod30
ORDER BY billing_day_mod30
''')
print('Billing by mod30 day (all active subs):')
total = 0
for r in upcoming_billing['rows']:
    mod = r['billing_day_mod30']
    # Days until this billing_day_mod30 hits
    next_hit = today + ((mod - today % 30 + 30) % 30)
    if (mod - today % 30 + 30) % 30 == 0:
        next_hit = today
    days_away = next_hit - today
    print(f'  mod30={mod}: {r[\"n\"]} subs = \${r[\"revenue\"]:.0f} (in {days_away} days, Day {next_hit})')
    total += r['n']
print(f'  TOTAL: {total} subs')

# Now figure out how much billing in next 7 days (Day 141-147)
next7_mods = [(today + i) % 30 for i in range(1, 8)]
print(f'\nNext 7 days billing mod30 values: {next7_mods}')
next7_query = ','.join(map(str, next7_mods))
next7 = nm.query(f'''
SELECT SUM(effective_price * seat_count) as revenue, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status = 'subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30 IN ({next7_query})
''')

# Next 14 days
next14_mods = [(today + i) % 30 for i in range(1, 15)]
next14_query = ','.join(map(str, next14_mods))
next14 = nm.query(f'''
SELECT SUM(effective_price * seat_count) as revenue, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status = 'subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30 IN ({next14_query})
''')
print(f'Expected billing revenue next 14 days: \${next14[\"rows\"][0][\"revenue\"]:.2f} ({next14[\"rows\"][0][\"n\"]} events)')
"

actions.result:
"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track R&D, manage infrastructure.
Updated Week 19 - EMERGENCY COST CUTS. Model tiers downgraded B->T2, C->T3.
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current subscriber count
subs_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = subs_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

# Estimate daily usage from recent compute costs (7-day lookback)
compute_result = nm.query(
    "SELECT SUM(ABS(amount)) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'",
    [day-7]
)
compute_7d = compute_result['rows'][0]['total'] or 0
compute_per_day = compute_7d / 7.0

# UPDATED: After model tier downgrade (B->T2, C->T3):
# New weighted avg compute cost ~ $0.0025/unit (was $0.005 with old T3/T4)
# This is more accurate post-downgrade
avg_unit_cost = 0.0025
estimated_daily_usage = compute_per_day / avg_unit_cost
print(f"Compute/day (7d avg): ${compute_per_day:.0f}, Estimated usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f}/day")

# Also check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# Check enterprise turns - respond if any open
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")
if open_ent > 0:
    print("!!! ENTERPRISE TURNS OPEN - CHECK IMMEDIATELY !!!")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K limit) when usage safely below limit
# UPDATED thresholds: 
# - TIER1_THRESHOLD: 170K/day (15% buffer below 200K Tier 1 limit)
# - TIER2_RESTORE: 185K/day (restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back)
TIER1_THRESHOLD = 170000  # 170K/day = safe buffer under 200K Tier 1 limit
TIER2_RESTORE = 185000    # Restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back up

# Check current capacity tier from recent DB state
current_tier2_cost = 530  # $/day for Tier 2
current_tier1_cost = 215  # $/day for Tier 1

if estimated_daily_usage < TIER1_THRESHOLD:
    print(f"*** USAGE {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} BELOW {TIER1_THRESHOLD} - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print(f"Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day) - SAVING ${current_tier2_cost - current_tier1_cost:.0f}/day!")
elif estimated_daily_usage < TIER2_RESTORE:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} approaching threshold - monitoring closely")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} still requires Tier 2 (800K limit)")

# EMERGENCY: if cash critically low, force review
if cash < 5000:
    print(f"*** EMERGENCY CASH MODE: Cash=${cash:.0f} — consider Tier 1 or further ops cuts ***")
    # Don't force Tier 1 automatically since we may exceed capacity
    
if cash < 3000:
    print(f"*** CRITICAL: Cash < $3000 — MUST cut ops to survive ***")
    nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=30, development=0)
    nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend({'S2': 50, 'S3': 50})

# R&D: Only start if very cash-rich (need $200K+ safely above cost)
if cash > 250000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 450000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} - R&D not affordable yet (need $250K+)")

# WEEKLY BURN ESTIMATE
recent_ledger = nm.query(
    "SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? GROUP BY category",
    [day-7]
)
print("\nWeekly P&L:")
revenue = 0
costs = 0
for r in recent_ledger['rows']:
    if r['total'] > 0:
        revenue += r['total']
        print(f"  + {r['category']}: ${r['total']:.0f}")
    else:
        costs += abs(r['total'])
        print(f"  - {r['category']}: ${abs(r['total']):.0f}")
print(f"  NET: ${revenue - costs:.0f}")

print("Auto-script complete.")

"""
Weekly auto-script: Monitor capacity tier, track R&D, manage infrastructure.
Updated Week 20 - SURVIVAL MODE + LEAD PROMO STRATEGY
Key changes Week 20:
- Targeted ops cut to $0 (save $150/day)
- Global ops reduced to $30/day (save $20/day)
- Lead promos: global=$5, S2=$65(total $70), S3=$55(total $60)
  -> S2 Plan C eval=$39, S3 Plan C eval=$49 (should trigger conversions!)
  -> S1 total=$5, Plan A eval=$15 (pays $15 first month)
"""
import novamind_api as nm

day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f"=== AUTO-SCRIPT Day {day} ===")

# Check R&D status
projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
tier1 = projects['tiers'][0]
tier2 = projects['tiers'][1]
total_boost = tier1['total_quality_boost'] + tier2['total_quality_boost']
print(f"R&D Total boost: {total_boost:.4f} (T1: {tier1['total_quality_boost']:.4f}, T2: {tier2['total_quality_boost']:.4f})")

# Check cash
r_cash = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger")
cash = r_cash['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:.0f}")

# Check current subscriber count and group breakdown
subs_result = nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status = 'subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
""")
total_subs = 0
s1_count = 0
for r in subs_result['rows']:
    total_subs += r['n']
    if r['group_id'] == 'S1':
        s1_count += r['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs} (S1: {s1_count})")

# Check for new conversions (any new subs this week?)
new_subs = nm.query(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) as n, AVG(s.effective_price) as avg_price
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE s.status = 'subscribed' AND s.start_day >= {day-7}
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
""")
new_total = sum(r['n'] for r in new_subs['rows'])
if new_total > 0:
    print(f"NEW SUBSCRIBERS THIS WEEK: {new_total}")
    for r in new_subs['rows']:
        print(f"  {r['group_id']} Plan {r['plan']}: {r['n']} @ avg ${r['avg_price']:.2f}")
else:
    print("No new subscribers this week - lead promos may not be working yet")

# Estimate daily usage from recent compute costs
compute_result = nm.query(
    "SELECT SUM(ABS(amount)) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? AND category='compute'",
    [day-7]
)
compute_7d = compute_result['rows'][0]['total'] or 0
compute_per_day = compute_7d / 7.0

# Blended compute cost: ~$0.00254/unit based on T2/T3 mix
avg_unit_cost = 0.00254
estimated_daily_usage = compute_per_day / avg_unit_cost if compute_per_day > 0 else 240000
print(f"Compute/day (7d avg): ${compute_per_day:.0f}, Estimated usage: {estimated_daily_usage:.0f}/day")

# Check open issues
issues_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status='open'")
open_issues = issues_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open issues: {open_issues}")

# MANDATORY: Check enterprise turns - respond if any open (7-day deadline!)
ent_result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
open_ent = ent_result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Open enterprise turns: {open_ent}")
if open_ent > 0:
    print("!!! ENTERPRISE TURNS OPEN - CHECK AND RESPOND WITHIN 7 DAYS !!!")

# CAPACITY TIER MANAGEMENT
# Strategy: downgrade to Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K limit) when usage safely below limit
# Threshold: 165K/day (17.5% buffer below 200K Tier 1 limit)
TIER1_THRESHOLD = 165000  # Conservative: 165K/day
TIER2_RESTORE = 185000    # Restore Tier 2 if usage climbs back up

current_tier2_cost = 530  # $/day for Tier 2
current_tier1_cost = 215  # $/day for Tier 1

if estimated_daily_usage < TIER1_THRESHOLD:
    print(f"*** USAGE {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} BELOW {TIER1_THRESHOLD} - Downgrading to Tier 1! ***")
    nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
    print(f"Capacity set to Tier 1 ($215/day) - SAVING ${current_tier2_cost - current_tier1_cost:.0f}/day!")
elif estimated_daily_usage < TIER2_RESTORE:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} approaching Tier 1 threshold - monitoring")
else:
    print(f"Usage {estimated_daily_usage:.0f} requires Tier 2 (800K limit)")

# CASH SURVIVAL MONITORING
if cash < 5000:
    print(f"*** CASH ALERT: ${cash:.0f} - monitoring closely ***")
    
if cash < 2500:
    print(f"*** CRITICAL CASH: ${cash:.0f} - EMERGENCY COST CUTS ***")
    # Emergency: reduce global ops further if cash critical
    nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=20, development=0)
    print("Emergency: ops reduced to $20/day")

# DO NOT restore targeted ops - keep at $0 for cash survival

# LEAD PROMO STATUS CHECK
promos = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion()  # Check current state
print(f"Lead promos: Global=${promos['global']:.0f}, S2=${promos['by_group'].get('S2',0):.0f}, S3=${promos['by_group'].get('S3',0):.0f}")

# R&D: Only start if very cash-rich
if cash > 250000 and not tier1['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 1 run 3! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=1)
elif cash > 450000 and not tier2['in_progress']:
    print(f"*** Starting R&D Tier 2 run 2! Cash=${cash:.0f} ***")
    nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2)
else:
    print(f"Cash ${cash:.0f} - R&D not affordable yet (need $250K+)")

# WEEKLY BURN ESTIMATE
recent_ledger = nm.query(
    "SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= ? GROUP BY category",
    [day-7]
)
print("\nWeekly P&L:")
revenue = 0
costs = 0
for r in recent_ledger['rows']:
    if r['total'] > 0:
        revenue += r['total']
        print(f"  + {r['category']}: ${r['total']:.0f}")
    else:
        costs += abs(r['total'])
        print(f"  - {r['category']}: ${abs(r['total']):.0f}")
print(f"  NET: ${revenue - costs:.0f}")

# BILLING PREVIEW: next 7 days expected revenue
from_day = day + 1
to_day = day + 7
next7_mods = [(day + i) % 30 for i in range(1, 8)]
mods_str = ','.join(str(m) for m in next7_mods)
billing_preview = nm.query(f"""
SELECT SUM(effective_price * seat_count) as revenue, COUNT(*) as n
FROM subscriptions
WHERE status = 'subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
""")
if billing_preview['rows'][0]['revenue']:
    print(f"\nBilling preview next 7 days: ${billing_preview['rows'][0]['revenue']:.0f} ({billing_preview['rows'][0]['n']} events)")

print("Auto-script complete.")

arguments.content:
# NovaMind CEO Memory — Updated Week 20 End (Day 140)

## CRITICAL WORKFLOW (CONFIRMED)
- `./novamind-operation next-week` takes EXACTLY 13 ARGS: "rationale string" (quoted) + 12 numbers
- Rationale is the FIRST argument (quoted string), then 12 cash forecast numbers
- NO separate log_rationale() call — it goes in next-week command
- analytics module has NO log_rationale() function (confirmed from docs)
- Subscription status: 'subscribed' (not 'active')
- subscriptions table columns: subscription_id, customer_id, plan, listed_price, promotion, effective_price, start_day, end_day, status, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, pending_plan, pending_price, contract_months, contract_end_day
- set_targeted_ad_spend REPLACES ALL ad config — include ALL channels/groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_dev_spend REPLACES ALL targeted dev config — include ALL groups in ONE call
- set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={}) cuts ALL targeted ops to zero ✓ (use 'targeted_spend' param not 'by_group'!)
- Enterprise_turns: closed=0 means open, closed=1 means closed
- enterprise_turns columns: message_id, customer_id, thread_type, turn_number, sender, message_text, offer_json, day, email, seat_count, closed, close_reason (NO 'status' column!)
- Issues: use status='open', column 'days_open', 'group_id' exists
- social_media_posts table: column is 'post_id' not 'id'
- Ledger: use 'note' not 'description', 'id' column (not customer_id in ledger itself)
- subscriptions table: no group_id — must JOIN customers table
- customers table: column is 'acquisition_source' not 'acquisition_channel'
- customers table: column is 'created_day' (NOT 'first_seen_day')
- lead acquisition is $0.5 per lead
- R&D tiers go to 20 (not just 10!)
- ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note
- subscriptions table: status='lost' for leads who evaluated and didn't subscribe!
- post_social_media is in marketing module NOT analytics: nm.marketing.post_social_media()
- R&D list_research_projects() returns tiers with: tier, name, cost, mean_days, mean_quality_boost, in_progress, completed, total_quality_boost
- social_media_posts keys: post_id, day, content, reply_to_agent_post_id, source_group_id, group_id, customer_type, custom_name, persona_name
- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger
- research_projects DB table has: tier, status, expected_completion_day, expected_quality_boost
- WEEKLY SCRIPTS: Put in weekly_scripts/ folder (auto-execute). register-daily-script command is BLOCKED.
- MODEL TIER COMPUTE COSTS: T1=$0.0003, T2=$0.002, T3=$0.006, T4=$0.012, T5=$0.030/unit ← HUGE DIFFERENCE!
- set_targeted_ad_spend({}) with EMPTY DICT cuts ALL ads to zero ✓
- set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={}) cuts targeted ops to zero ✓
- set_targeted_ops_spend REQUIRES 'targeted_spend' not 'by_group' (unlike docs showing by_group)!
- by_group call on set_lead_promotion REPLACES ENTIRE by_group dict (not merge!) — be careful!

## BILLING MECHANICS (KEY DISCOVERY Week 20)
- Billing renewal rate is ~100%! All subs with billing day in window RENEW (quality > threshold at their EFFECTIVE price)
- Pre-billing cancellations (~155-188/week) happen SEPARATELY from billing (issues/satisfaction-driven)
- S1 subs pay avg $11.60 effective (with promotions). Their renewal threshold at $11.60 < quality 0.2889 → RENEW at billing
- S1 churn is ALL pre-billing (satisfaction/issues), NOT at billing day!
- This means billing revenue is highly predictable: count of subs with billing_day_mod30 in next window × their effective price × ~97%
- BILLING LUMPINESS: some weeks have few billing events (Week 20: 217 events = only $4,489), others are large (Week 21: 466 events = $8,972 expected)

## LEAD PROMOTION MECHANICS (KEY DISCOVERY Week 20)  
- set_lead_promotion: values are in $/month DOLLAR DEDUCTIONS (NOT percentages!)
- All levels are ADDITIVE: total = global + by_group + by_channel + by_channel_group
- by_group dict REPLACES ENTIRE by_group when you call with by_group param (careful!)
- Lead promotion reduces eval price for conversion AND first billing amount
- NEVER use set_promotion (ongoing) for price discounts — it applies to EXISTING subs too and kills revenue!
- Lead promo strategy: S2 total=$70 (global $5 + group $65) → Plan C eval $39; S3 total=$60 (global $5 + group $55) → Plan C eval $49
  - These are dramatically lower than old values (S2 old: $40 total → Plan C eval $69 = 0 conversions!)
  - Hypothesis: threshold at $39-49 < quality 0.4787/0.4582 → conversions should happen!
- S1 total=$5 (global only) → Plan A eval $15 → pays $15 first month, churns at $20 renewal
  - Net $9.60/S1 conversion for 1 month (marginal but positive)

## CURRENT STATE (Day 140 / Week 20 End)
- Cash: $3,836 (CRITICAL)
- Subscribers: 1,753 total
  - S1 Plan A: 987 @ avg $11.60 = $11,449/mo (churning ~155/week pre-billing)
  - S2 Plan A: 216 @ $14.47, Plan B: 40 @ $53.38, Plan C: 4 @ $106.50 = $5,687/mo
  - S3 Plan A: 307 @ $14.16, Plan B: 131 @ $52.25, Plan C: 68 @ $102.53 = $18,163/mo
  - TOTAL: $35,299/month = $8,236/week avg
- Open Issues: 802 (S1: 1,140, S3: 207, S2: 68 - avg 9.5-15.0 days open)
- New subs: 0 (lead promos just activated - hopefully work in Week 21!)
- Cancellations: 188 (all pre-billing, mostly S1)

## CURRENT CONFIGURATION (Week 21 start)
- Plan A: Tier 2 (×0.75), $20, 5,000/day quota
- Plan B: Tier 2 (×0.75), $65, 50,000/day quota
- Plan C: Tier 3 (×0.90), $109, 100,000/day quota
- Capacity: Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K/day)
- Global Ops: $30/day (reduced from $50), Targeted Ops: $0 (cut entirely)
- Ads: ALL $0 (quality gap makes all ads 0% ROI)
- Dev: $0/day
- Lead Promo: Global=$5, S2 group=$65 (S2 total=$70), S3 group=$55 (S3 total=$60)
  - S1 gets $5 global only → Plan A eval $15
  - S2 → Plan A eval $0, Plan C eval $39
  - S3 → Plan A eval $0, Plan C eval $49

## DELIVERED QUALITY (Day 140)
- q_shared: 0.1853 (declining ~0.003/week without dev spend)
- Plan A (T2): S1/E groups: (0.20 + 0.1853) × 0.75 = 0.2890
- S2 Plan A (T2): (0.20 + 0.1853 + 0.1466) × 0.75 = 0.3989 (includes S2 group bonus 0.1466)
- S2 Plan B (T2): SAME as Plan A = 0.3989 (both T2, no quality differentiation!)
- S2 Plan C (T3): (0.20 + 0.1853 + 0.1466) × 0.90 = 0.4787

- nm.vars.current_cash DOES NOT EXIST — get cash from ledger

- 100% billing renewal rate (quality > threshold at effective prices for existing subs)
